Review_Text: Great quality and affordable price for local food, they serve both Thai and Burmese food 🥘 I highly recommend the stir fried with sweet and sour sauce chicken. Yummy! …

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Wonderful food, very authentic. The tea leaf and tamarind salads are some of the best I've had.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Delicious. The tea leaf salad was amazing! Also would recommend the eggplant curry and stir-fry peppers

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Excellent food. Eggplant curry is a must to try. That's our favorite dish. It is a famouse local restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Been eating here for years and years. One of my favorite restaurants. The Burmese food is amazing, prices are more than reasonable, staff are lovely.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Good Burmese food, fast, relatively cheap. Food can be a bit too oily sometimes, though. Loved their chicken biryani and their tamarind leaves salad.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Tasty Myanmar food. When you see the name of the restaurant at the entrance, you will be doubtful if Myanmar food is available or not because they wrote 'Mynmar' instead of 'Myanmar.'

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: The tea leaf and tamarind leaf salad are to die for. Do yourself a favor and order them spicy and get ready for the rollercoaster of flavor baby !

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: My favourite restaurant. Best food in Nimman area. Get the eggplant curry, tea leaf salad and mustard soup. So good

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: I agree with the other poster. This place seems really dirty compared to the other few shops next to it and the food is always served lukewarm. The taste is probably good, but I had to toss the food as this place just seemed like food poisoning waiting to happen.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Amazing food! If it was three times the price, it'd still be worth the value. I've been coming here for the last 3 years, Greg and consistent quality!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: A super casual and quirky place with delicious food. The young tamarind leaf salad is delicious.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Lovely little place. Sweet staff and great food. Very reasonably priced too.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: The food was delicious! Authentic and cheap prices. Great environment and kind people.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Very good tastes and reasonable price👍 …

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Review_Text: Really nice Burmese food, small portions for low money, so you can order and try a lot of different dishes. Perfect!

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Uncle Pan Thai Food offers great value for local Thai food. Everything was fresh, flavorful, and delicious. The prices were very reasonable, and we truly enjoyed the meal.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text:
A really delicious little local spot that opens early 😋
I had the crispy pork and chinese kale for only 70 baht, and it one of the best versions of this dish I've had!
Will definitely be back to try more.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: We got the fried rice (70 baht) and green curry rice (60 baht) and we love it! We came during lunch time and a bunch of locals were eating here as well! Love this little local spot!

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: ผัดไทยร้านนี้อร่อยมากค่ะ แม่ค้าเลยก็ใจดี🥰 ไปเชียงใหม่ทีไรต้องไปกินตลอด

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: I’m leaving this review because I truly hope the owners can keep this place running for a long time. 🙂 It’s impeccably clean, the food is delicious, and the prices are very reasonable! I visit this restaurant every time I’m in Chiang Mai—that alone should tell you how much this place is worth a visit.🙏

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: I just randomly walked in and found that this is a super tasty and nice restaurant！Uncle pan cooks so well. I love the chilli fish so much. Tom Yum Gong is also amazing，sour and spicy enough，超级下饭哈哈哈哈哈哈哈

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Simple local restaurant but great food. Very local. The Pork Panang Curry was delicious and the pad thai also. Great option if you are in the area

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text:
A casual local eatery! Most dishes start at 50B, and shrimp dishes are about 15B extra. The owner couple is very kind, and although it’s a local spot, it’s quite clean!
If you’re staying nearby, make sure to drop by!

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: อาหาร บริการ บรรยากาศพื้นๆเพราะเป็นร้านที่ตั้งราคาให้คนไทยกิน แต่ถ้ารวมทุกอย่างเข้าด้วยกันเมื่อเทียบกับราคาอาหารจานละ 50 - 100 บ. สำหรับนักท่องเที่ยวแล้วก็สามารถให้สี่ดาวได้เลย

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: If you are looking for a quick Thai dish with reasonable price, this is a good choice. It locates in small alley next to Hilside Condo II or opposite Nimman Soi 13. The owner is uncle who cook food dish by dish. The taste is authentic local street Thai food from simple menus like Fried rice or Stir Fried meat with basil. They offer free self service water and soup. If you come during lunch time, you will need to wait a bit or better come in off peak hour.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: อร่อยสุดในย่านนี้ ราคาไม่แพง มีเมนูหลากหลาย น้ำซุปบริการตัวเองตักได้ไม่อั้น แนะนำเมนูหมูกรอบครับ

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text:
Cheap, but awesome local restaurant near nimman road!

you can have everything you want under 100  thb here, and the taste is good too,
the owner is really kind, always showed a smile to me.

It opens from 7:30 am, so if you looking for pretty good breakfast retaurant, this is it!

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Uncle pan and his wife are great people, honest, fast and cheap , amazing location in nimman area with fair price and authentic Thai flavor , thank you 🙏🏼 see u soon again!! 100% recommended ❤️

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: There are several local stores next to each other here and I think the food here is the best among them. However, this store is actually least visible compare to others. They are nice and the food is very good and cheap.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text:
Great value, well executed authentic street food. ~50-60 baht for a dish. Pictured are the crispy fried pork with basil and the pad thai.

I wouldn't hesitate to eat here every day if I stayed in the area. Hard to beat the value. Get the crispy fried pork with basil!

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: ข้าวอร่อยทำค่อนข้างเร็ว แค่ถ้ามาช่วงกลางวันอาจรอนานนิดหน่อยเพราะลูกค้าเยอะ ราคาดี ปริมาณอิ่มกำลังดี มีน้ำซุปให้ด้วย

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Every dish was great, especially crispy pork with basil. Morning glory is a great side dish. The owners work very hard and are friendly. We came back twice. The price is very good too.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: This place is not standing out visually among others, but food is so good here! And so cheap!

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: This is by far my favorite simple Thai restaurant and it's right next door to my condo Hillside2 he has all of your favorite Thai food recipes fresh cooked to order. At a very reasonable price free cool water clean comfortable sit down restaurant we highly recommend

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: I love this Couple! Food is fast, delicious, and plentiful! I've been a near daily customer for almost 2 years. I can't remember the last time I was in a Grocery store! 🤣🤣, I just go downstairs to Uncle Pan.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Local Thai food with English menu and pictures so can easily order. Food tasted good, 115 baht for 2 dishes and bottle of water.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Excellent food, and the nicest chef! Can definitely recommend, it doesn't look like much but its great

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง

Review_Text: Good food with good price, so so delicious, highly recommended!!! Uncle owner speaks some English so it's convenient for foreigners. No English on the signboard so you need to see google pictures to recognize this place. Thanks to google reviewers we found this great place for dinner, much appreciated!

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Very delicious spicy fried pork with basil leaves for a cheap price.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text:
Great authentic local Thai food with quality ingredient, tasty at dirt cheap price.  Seafood dish just 2 usd, other dishes about 1-1.5 usd, and a cup of free soup with every order.  There's menu with pics, you can easily order your favorite dish.  This place is made-to-order kind of restaurant.

*** 2022 Update ***
After four years, and my absent from Chiang Mai for 2 years, dining here again is quite deja vu.  Everything look and feel the same, the taste is the same great taste for Thai local cuisine.  Free soup still here for every dish, if you don't get it just ask.  We'll keep coming back here every chance we get.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text:

Classic but tasty thai street food, very good Pad Thai and curry dishes. Highly recommended for quick daily lunch/dinner.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง

Review_Text: A delicious, clean, and friendly local restaurant. I had a hearty meal.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Great value and great taste. Plus, the hygiene is top-notch.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: Delicious and affordable, highly recommended!!

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: It's delicious and friendly.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: I went there on 8/2 with great difficulty, but they were closed.

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: very good food, 5/5

Restaurant_Name: ลุงพัน อาหารตามสั่ง
Review_Text: This is a famous restaurant for hollow vegetables.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Seriously one of the most underrated rated 5-star Vegan restaurants in Chiang Mai!! Anchan is a family run business which has the food dialed in perfectly.  If you’re looking for a fabulous veggie rich meal loaded with flavors, drop in to Anchan.

The Panang curry will literally knock your socks off! We loved the veggie stir fry and the tofu pepper and garlic.

It’s located upstairs with a wonderful open air balcony and great view of a calm street.  The family is very kind and caring.  We’ve eaten here over a dozen times during our stay and have never been disappointed.  ♥️

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This place is so amazing and reasonably priced. There were so many delicious options and we decided on pad Thai butterfly pea noodles with tofu and mushrooms, tofu pepper and garlic stir fry, Thai papaya salad, and panaeng curry macaroni. All the dishes were so flavorful with my favorite being the panaeng curry macaroni. The staff was very welcoming and friendly. The four dishes, 2 soda waters, and 2 bowls of rice came out to 530 baht! We will definitely be back!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
We visited this vegetarian restaurant and really enjoyed the atmosphere — it was pleasant, relaxed, and inviting. The vibe of the place was definitely a highlight and made the experience comfortable.

As for the food, it was a bit mixed. Their signature dish with rice was actually very tasty and stood out positively. However, aside from that, the rest of the dishes were just okay. Nothing was bad, but nothing really impressed us either, and we didn’t end up finishing everything.

Overall, it’s a nice place to sit and enjoy the atmosphere, but the food itself didn’t fully meet our expectations.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I've been eating here for years and never tried their fried tempeh with tamarind sauce. It is unbelievably delicious 😋. I also had red curry Tempeh which an all time favorite of mine. As always, bursting with flavor ✨️🙏🏽

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
TL;DR: Food was nice, but I wouldn’t say highly recommend — especially if you’re low on cash. There are lots of other great spots around anyway.

The food was generally mild and tasty. The curry was good, and the taro fritters were especially delicious — not something you find often, and the portions were generous too.

But… the restaurant don’t accept credit card🫠 They have a QR option, but it didn’t work with GLN (kept showing a message saying it’s restricted due to government policy?). I had to rush out to withdraw cash. Even most street stalls accept GLN, so I was a bit surprised that a restaurant didn’t…

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great take-away feast! We chose pumpkin red curry and tofu pepper garlic stir-fry. Both came with brown rice, and it was all so delicious! Lots of tofu, loved it. Highly recommend and hope we get the chance to return.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Extremely recommended!
Very tasty and healthy food, you can tell its made with love from the heart, big portions and small prices! Massaman curry is creamy and keeps you looking forward to one more spoon. Pad Si Ew is colorful and well-balanced with many textures.

Atmosphere and design is pretty and relaxed, piano jazz in the background alongside the pans and pots clanking together.

Staff was friendly. Menu is easy to understand and fully vegetarian.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Some restaurants in Chiang Mai don’t just show up—you have to look for them. And Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant is one of those secret spots tucked away in a quiet alley, up on the second floor. I almost thought I was lost, but then I spotted the blue flower sign (FYI, "Anchan" means butterfly pea flower)—mystery solved!

Upstairs, it’s a cozy open space with wooden tables, gentle fans, and the kind of chill vibe that makes you want to stay for hours. The staff welcomed me with warm smiles and casual English chit-chat. Most of the diners were Westerners, all looking like they just came out of a meditation retreat—very zen.

I ordered their green curry with wild mushrooms and brown rice, and wow—the flavor was rich but clean. No MSG, no overpowering sauces, just natural goodness and a lot of fresh veggies. It felt like something your yoga instructor would approve of.

For drinks, I had their butterfly pea herbal tea, a gorgeous indigo-lavender shade that basically begged for a photo. It had a light citrus twist and was surprisingly refreshing even without any sweetener.

One thing I loved is how they keep it real: no artificial colorings, no heavy seasonings, just good food that feels kind to your body. It’s the kind of place you go to when your stomach needs a break from all the spicy street food but you still want something delicious.

So if you’re into quiet spots, clean eats, and hidden gems, Anchan Vegetarian is 100% worth the walk. Just follow the blue flower and trust the process.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Amazing vegan and vegetarian food! The staff are lovely and understand which dishes are vegan or can be made vegan. It is upstairs- just look for the sign!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Absolutely fantastic food! At first I thought was a little pricey but once you see the size of the massive portions you’ll see it’s reasonably priced. We ordered the tofu cashew stir fry (5 stars), pumpkin red curry (4.5 stars), and Tom kah (4.5 hours). Would order any of them again. We took the soup to go bc it was simply too much food for 2 ppl and I can really throw it back. Order 1 dish per person and you’ll be happy

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Absolutely one of our favorite vegetarian/organic restaurants in Chiang Mai!
We have been going to this restaurant for almost 10 years now, and have taken many friends for dinner here that have been equally amazed with the great menu and the high quality and unique flavors of the food.
They have mostly traditional Thai dishes, but pasta dishes and salads are also on the menu.
The portions are always really large, so arrive here hungry. More food comes out than what you expect every time.
Prices are moderate, but it’s a great value and very much worth it for the super fresh and delicious food.
They also have smoothies and desserts, which vary daily.
They don’t serve alcoholic drinks or beer.
We will definitely keep returning here for both lunch and dinner, and recommend it to everyone.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I've been consistently coming here for over four years and am always pleased with the food quality and consistency. Every dish is delicious and I am sure you will find a favorite as I have. My go to dish is the pumpkin curry, it is fantastic. The veggies are all fresh and organic and the curry is wonderful. Do yourself a favor and eat here whether you are vegetarian or not.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Tucked about one block walk down a quiet side street is this unassuming restaurant on Floor 2 of an old shop house.  Google maps brought us to the front of he building, but it required seeing a sign next the stairwell door to find the restaurant. Atmosphere is inviting with colorful murals adorning the walls and rustic furniture. We took a seat near the balcony which was entirely open to cool night breeze next to the mountain.  A large menu which contained sophisticated curries and traditional Thai dishes made the choices difficult.  Everything we ordered was delicious with nicely blended flavors and spice in the red curry (feel the heat but not sweating level) and noodles and rice were tasty, as were the healthy drinks. Would definitely return - despite the large number of very good vegetarian and vegan restaurants in Chiang Mai - this one stood out from the crowd for taste and quality.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Local food adapted to western taste (not spicy). Great variety of dishes. Massive portions! (Order 1 dish per person and you will have plenty). Great if you can seat by the balcony:)

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Such tasty food. If I could eat this every day I’d become vegetarian.
The Massaman Curry had big pieces of tofu and potato which made it really filling.

I wish I hadn’t discovered this restaurant on my last day in Chiang Mai. I’ll be back one day though to try the rest of their menu.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I tried the green curry and I love it. The price range is at the higher range among the vegetarian restaurants that I visited in Chiang Mai. But it is still worth the price due to the organic vegetables. The atmosphere is nice as well. Ideal place for family or friends gathering. The restaurant is located at the 1st floor. You will need to climb stairs to get there.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Being vegan is hard when you don't speak Thai. So finding this place was great.

Took the stairs to the 2nd floor. Where I was greeted as soon as I walked in.

English speaking staff.

The wall was nicely painted. The building is dated.

The food is large portion sizes so be aware that you can share.

The food is Flavorful!! The drinks are good. Stop by and give them a try.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
First time trying the food here. Love the tasty food! It's a cosy little place and really cooling due to the open air and balcony window.

Anyway, definitely will be back to try more from the menu.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Would recommend. The prices are very reasonable and we found the portions to be enough for two people. There is a smaller menu with various curries, stir fried and rice dishes. We've tried the pumpkin curry, massaman, Thai basil fried rice and the stir fried onion flowers. You get brown rice so be prepared as it's a bit nutty. You can get dishes changed to vegan if required. It's a nice setting across from an apartment and up a small set of stairs.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Best vegetarian experience for me in Chiang Mai. Every single menu item I have tried was a favorite.

Tasty, health giving food. More powerful than any vaccine, this is true natural medicine.

Come and enjoy, even if vegetarian food is not your thing!

(delicious)

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Wonderful Thai food, 100% vegetarian located in Nimman neighbourhood. Please note they are closed on Tuesdays and Wednesdays!! Best food I had in Chiang Mai.. they can cater to vegan and Jain (no allium/no onion no garlic).

I had the green curry with vegetables and mango salad. Both are freshly made and delicious!!! The salad portion is big, can easily be shared by 2 people.

Prices are slightly more expensive but I enjoyed every bit. Would recommend this place for anyone looking for wonderful Thai food while in Chiang Mai. Specially for vegetarians and Jains, look no further and ensure you eat at least one meal here!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
It is an excellent place for a thai vegetarian meal. We were there for lunch yesterday and today.  The dishes we ordered were varied, and the tastes were quite authentic and tasty.  The chef accommodated our request for dishes to be spicy.  We also had the Lime smoothie and the Papaya and lime smoothie. Both were great in rounding up our meals.
The restaurant is a   minute walk from the Yesterday Hotel.  Google Map will get you there. The restaurant is also on the second level from the street accessible by a side staircase.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
best vegetarian restaurant i’ve been to so far in chiang mai. super impressed with the variety of vegetables, mushrooms, soy bean/ tofu/ tempeh dishes.
😋🍄 🥦🥕🍅🌽🌶️🍠🫑🫛🥬

i would really recommend every thing we ate, but my favourite was the tempeh fried rice and pumpkin red curry. the kale with tofu is delicious too.

fill your belly up with this well-nourishing experience!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Anchan is a GREAT restaurant on the 2nd floor (walkup) in the Nimman area.

So many things looked good on the menu, but I opted for the fried banana flower salad and massaman curry with a butterfly pea flower drink (anchan means butterfly pea flower).

It was all so crazy colorful and delicious! I loved being there.

Update:

The food is still excellent!

Their kitchen is so open, which I very much appreciate, but I do not appreciate being treated rudely by the cook and being told not to take pictures.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: The staff is unsmiling and unhelpful. That aside, the food. Ah. It literally sings when they set it in front of you, full of color and zest! Another reviewer said overpriced for the product, but I haven't had so many different kinds of really fresh vegetables in one dish. We won't be returning, because we want to go somewhere we feel values our business, but it's definitely a must try, especially for vegetarians. Note- menu varies week to week, the resturant is located up the stairs on the second floor and tofu is in most dishes (even if not listed).

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Their coconut curry is AMAZING. highly recommend. Pay attention to their opening days, we came here 2x before and it was not open. The food and service are great, very healthy and yummy food.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Excellent dishes, especially love masarman curry, good flavor but not overly strong. Staffs/owner are no more unfriendly as some reviewers mentioned. No air condition and on 2nd floor, can get a little hot in March/April. Will definitely go back again.

Had been back a few times. The food has been consistently good. Definitely one of our favorite restaurants in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: If you look for vegetarian in Thai style this may be the choice. Every table had some tourist and it took little time to prepare. We ordered Anchan style fried rice and vegetable with cashew fried rice and Tofu cashew nut sweet n sour. All were smaller, stir fry with little different mix. I would recommend this for stir fry lovers. Not a bad place.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Best vegetarian meal in chiang mai for sure. We ordered so much and ate so much because it was so delicious. The miso eggplant is a must-try and the green curry is wonderful. Also the potato curry powder stir fry was awesome flavours

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
One of the best vegan restaurants. Small restaurant, but the food was delicious. Masaman curry, Veggie fried rice,  Pod Thai noodles all are good.  Papaya juice was refreshing.

No air-conditioning, but food did make up for it. Close to Maya and 1 Nimman malls.

Restaurant is upstairs, easy to miss if you don't pay attention.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
The one and only Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant is dazzling tastebuds and leaving customers with positive food memories.

The food here is absolutely amazing 😍!!! Everything is made fresh to order. So expect the highest quality and freshest preparations.

I consider Anchan to be the leader of plant-based community here in Chiang Mai. No matter how time passes by the food and flavor profiles don't change.

The Chef, Aye, is a master of flavors and presentation of culinary delights. If you have allergens or particular dietary restriction, Aye is able to accommodate you without sacrificing the authentic flavors. I appreciate that very much!

Truly his preparations are made with love and passion.

What you won't find here is fast food or prepared meals that have been sitting for hours and reheated. Always always fresh.

Love and appreciation to the Anchan family.

I am a traveling plant-based chef specializing in food science and flavorology. Check out my other reviews on Google Maps.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
BRO. This MAY BE the best curry I’ve EVER had. And I’ve lived in Asia for a hot minute.

Dear LORD whatever they put in here is magical. I got the green curry and my buddy got the Massaman. He filled up so I ate half of his and they were both SO good I honestly couldn’t pick one if there was a gun to my head.

OK maybe that’s over dramatic. I’d probably go green. BUT STILL.

I told the staff thank you so much they got tired of me by the end because I couldn’t stop thanking them for making such delicious food.

I could continue. But I won’t. Just…go.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
This is the worst restaurant I've ever had 2 months after I came to Chiang Mai. Stir-fried vegetables - sweet enough to eat. Vegetarian rice — sweet enough, I suspect I was drinking cola. Mango salad - acid to throw directly.

Waitress bad attitude came and I theory that the food here are put sugar are normal. I paid THB455. Money can be pay it worst food. But so bad food and bad attitude women really make everyone not interested in spending again. We live in Toronto of Canada. Travel for meals all over the world. All the things I've never seen are sweet  rice and sweet Vegetable dishes in all Qiang Mai , Thailand , all of the world.

If the owner think my taste is wrong please  pay and to go different restaurant to  try it.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Amazing thai food, all vegetarian with many vegan options as well. The portions were really big. You can adjust the spice level to your liking.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Super cute little hole in the wall restaurant! Neither my boyfriend or I are vegetarian but we heard good things about this place and had to check it out. Cozy atmosphere, it was quite busy when we showed up but the service was still great. Had the curry, corn fritters and sweet and sour tofu stir fry and it was all absolutely delicious!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Food wise is ok. Nothing to shout about. Tasted two fried dishes. The fried noodle is a bit salty for me. Big portion to compare with local pricing, plus they cooked with organic vegetables(If it is so, it is considered cheap). No homemade chilli sauce....hmm..mm.....Note: Located on first floor and no lift for disabled access.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great Thai vegetarian food, consistently delicious, fast service, usually tables are available, prices are reasonable. Good drinks and smoothies.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Very good curry with vegetables. Fast and hot. Not too spicy. The papaya salad was pretty bland, you need to compensate for the no fish oil, extra seasoning is needed. Nice clean place. Upstairs. Getting crowded at 6 pm. Watch your head on the overhanging table lights, ouch! 200 baht for soup, rice and a smoothie. The owners put a lot of passion into their food.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Absolutely delicious vegetarian food! We had some curry potatoes and garlic tofu which were very tasty ✨

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: One of my favourite restaurants in Chiang Mia. The portions are great, the flavour is exceptional and we Have kept coming back again and again! Highly recommended!!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I’m not vegan but I love this place. Huge portions. You could easily order 1 dish grab 2 rice and be filled up. Recommend the yellow curry and the black pepper

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Pumpkin red curry was amazing!! Huge portion and delicious flavors, I can’t wait to try more of the curries here

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Had a lovely meal here. The restaurant is down a quiet alley and had a few seats available when we arrived, around 715 pm.

After being seated we were given the menu and a pen. It's a paper menu where those seated at the table select their dish and number of dishes.

Menu selection is huge with curries, soups, stir fry, fried rices, fried treats, and drinks. All sounded so good, all are vegetarian with some dishes being vegan. Almost all the dishes are vegetarian versions of popular Thai dishes.

After ordering we got our drinks and food came out after about 20 or so minutes. The kitchen is open to the restaurant with 3-4 people cooking. As it's not a huge kitchen be prepared to wait a bit for your food if it's busy.

The dishes themselves we're excellent. Tons of super fresh veggies and seasoned well. If you're a meat eater, you really won't miss it. Portions are also large, we could have easily split a curry. They also serve local brown and mixed rice, not the usual white rice found throughout Asia.

Prices were fair for the area with mains being around 120 to 180 baht. For the quality of food and portion size the prices are fantastic.

Staff were friendly and prompt, and the general atmosphere of the place can be described as welcoming and healthy!

All in all, a great meal.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Located off of Nimman Rd. in the narrow Soi 8.  If you look to your left as going into the Soi you will find it on your left.  It's located on the second floor above Mana Co-Working & Reading Space.

In general, Anchan is normally very consistent with their food.  So much that many of the menu items taste extremely similar to one another, no matter how many times they re-arrange the weekly menu.

Yes, it is a bit expensive by local Thai standards.  Although I no longer eat here, it did serve it's purpose as a go-to-eatery, while searching out better alternatives.

One big let down that I hope will change one day soon is whenever you order delivery, although it’s a convenience they warp EVERYTHING in plastic bags.  Aside from the plastic pollution, the plastic itself can cause harm as it’s absorbed into the food.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This seems to be what passes for as a mid priced place in Chiang Mai. The portion sizes are generous. I haven't tried a whole lot of food from here, but from what I have tried, I would like to try more!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Had a fantastic meal at Anchan. I honestly think this was one of the best vegan meals I've had so far in Chiang Mai. We got the green curry with veggies and tofu, yellow curry potatos, and long eggplant in thai miso sauce. Wow. Such great flavors. I can't wait to come back and can't beleive I haven't visited sooner. Highly recommend!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: The food was good but a little bit bland, could have been more spicy and salty. The Som Tam was to sweet in my opinion. But all together still a very good restaurant experience. Large portions and good service, too.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I’m writing this while Im still at the dining table. Clean place, fresh and tasty food, generous portion. One of our favorite vegan places in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I have been searching fo the best vegan/vegetarian food in Chiang Mai. I think I can conclude my study after eating at Anchan. The owner is a amazing chef and the restaurant is tucked away from the street noise. I will be back everyday! I tried the Panage Curry and the corn fritters. Both simply delicious.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Finally a good curry. Thai curries are everywhere but it's not easy to find a good one. I got the tempeh red curry and requested pumpkin.

Thick coconut soup base, red curry paste, finally adequate basil leaves, lemongrass- galangaly, sweetness for coconut milk, pumpkin and carrots, a hint of spicy.  Dreamy curry and a big portion. I thought I'd be taking half home (spoiler: I ate everything).

Ps- no Aircon... No problem, you're sweating outside anyway. Let the curry make you sweat even more. Nothing an icey drink can't fix.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
We came here after a dissapointing lunch in Nimman at another restaurant and were blown away. We thought that the food in this neighborhood was over hyped but quickly realized that this restaurant lived up to everything we read! Highly recommend visiting!

Came back for a second time within 24 hours of the first meal. My wife loved the Cashew Nut Thai Salad while I loved the Pad Thai Mushroom and the Crispy Tofu in Tamarind Sauce. The Long Egg Plant Miso Stirfry was also delicious!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: It was pretty average.  The butterfly pea flower noodles pad thai was good but nothing extraordinary.  Green mango salad was ok.. Around 150baht a plate.  Kitchen closes quite early - at 8.15pm

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I am so happy I tried this restaurant! It was absolutely delicious and I was happy to see they serve kombucha! The curry was really good quantity and fresh! I would definitely recommend this to anyone looking for authentic vegetarian Thai food in Chiang Mai!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Fresh and delicious food~
I love everything that I ordered
recommend this place 👌🏽
정말 맛있어요~
รสชาติร้านนี้ดี กินแล้วแฮปปี้
ราคาต่อจานประมาณ120 ราคาสูงนิดๆ
แต่ไม่ได้กินบ่อย ก็นานๆกินที

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: While the menu is limited, the dishes are reasonably priced and generous, and very tasty. A great veg option if you're in the area. Access through staircase so people with reduced mobility should avoid the place.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
I've eaten Thai food everyday for the last week in Krabi and Chiang Mai and weekly in NYC's Hells Kitchen Thai food district. This is hands down the MOST DELICIOUS Thai food I've ever experienced. We walked in for lunch and every table was taken. They cook each other fresh so be prepared to wait a little bit if you come in during a busy time - it's worth it!!! I'm bummed it's closed the next 2 days we are in Chiang Mai. Forever etched in my soul, amazing food.

I ordered the massaman curry and tofu pepper and garlic stirfry. Both were vegan and gluten-free. Also got the refreshing tamarind iced tea. So thankful for Anchan for having so many options. My partner, an avid lover of Chicken, got the Egg Red Curry and Thai Basil Fried Rice and also believes this is the best Thai he has ever had.

Vegetarian/vegan or not, you must try this place during your time in Chiang Mai. Will definitely need to come back to Chiang Mai and Thailand just for Anchan.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delectable dishes served in a cool, relaxed and airy dining room. Warm and welcoming family-run restaurant. Very reasonably priced. (Oh, bah humbug to those who complain about paying around $3.60 for a generous plate of perfect Pad Thai obviously made with love that you know damn well you'd get thrown at you in the States for $7.99). This recipe's packed with firm tofu with crispy edges, scrambled egg, tender scallions, pan-seared mushrooms, juicy lime, sweet carrot slices, peanuts, noodles, and crisp bean sprouts. I love that they made it mild for me and happily handed me red chili flakes to add as desired. Paired with a refreshing berry soda smoothie. Felt almost like joining a local family for a meal (while a Whitney Houston song played on the stereo)! Share takeout at home with friends with limited mobility since there are only stairs to enter. I brought home Massaman Tempeh Curry with brown rice to dig into later, smells divine. Thanks new friends at Anchan! Peace, Starr 🙏

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: The food was tasty, but a bit too sweet for us.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Thai food with the most vegetables I’ve seen in Thailand. Great portions for a good price. And just delicious!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Came here hungry! Ordered a spicy stir fry and a massaman curry not thinking the portions would be has huge as in the picture. Scarfed then down. Both delicious, though the ground peanuts in the massaman curry tasted a bit on the dated side. Nevertheless, the owners know what they're doing. Will be back.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Incredible little place! Super fresh and made to order. Clearly family run and operated-super clean and well taken care of. Fast and attentive service. I’ll be back!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delicious vegan Thai dishes! The massaman curry is always a good choice & the prices are fair for portions. The menu is huge so there’s something for everyone. Highly recommend!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Excellent organic vegetaria food, I visit it every year when I go to Changmai. Papaya salad are delicious and fresh. Pad Thai with butterfly pea noodles are special.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: The food is pretty good, although to be honest given the prices I was expecting much more. Most dishes are over 100 baht but in my opinion this is not made up for in the quality or quantity of food. The restaurant itself is not so relaxing especially as it was dependant upon fans to keep cool while I was there. Staff seemed friendly enough. Personally I think you can find much better and cheaper veggie/vegan places if you're willing to just go out of Nimman.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Very nice vegetarian food. Portions are good. Affordable price. Veggies are fresh. Opposite side of Yantasri apartment. Recommended.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Head up the steps of this tucked away restaurant with a quiet ambience but welcoming staff. English menus are available here to help you choose the excellent vegetarian fare on offer.

Don't be afraid to try the Pad Thai Jays as normal as they sound, they are great. One dish is usually more than enough for one person and usually works out to around 120 baht. I can also recommend the smoothies, particularly the banana, honey and coconut, served with environmentally friendly silicone straws.

There's a balcony overlooking modern residential Chiang Mai if the weather is good. Fans blow to keep the mostly tourist clientele cool here. Highly recommended if you're vegetarian, or not.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Best red curry we've had in Thailand! Nice atmosphere and friendly service. Would love to go back and try everything else on their extensive vegetarian/vegan menu.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Really good food, cooked with attention - especially the green curry. The portions are great and the venue very peaceful. Will come back again!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Reliable and affordable place for veg/vegan food. Generous portions. Blue pea flower pad thai was alright. Paneng curry was excellent. Banana coconut smoothie was very sweet. Strong wifi and great setting to get some work done while you eat.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Amazing food, blown away and I'm a chef. I recommend the fried egg red curry and papaya salad (the best one I've had in thailand)

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This place is one of our favorites in Chiang Mai. You just can't beat the freshness and the taste is  a bit elevated from what you find other places. Portions are large, so you can justify the slightly higher prices. Highly recommend butterfly noodle pad Thai, massaman curry, green curry omelette and papaya lime smoothie. Vegan and vegetarian friendly and they will ask if you want egg or not.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: After many disapointing meals in Nimman this has become our favorite place even though we are not vegetarians. They have a wide variety of set meals that change each week. The staff are all really friendly and helpful to pickout what to eat. We highly recommend it.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
When I ordered the Thai basil fried rice, the waitress asked if egg was ok, I said “no, add extra tofu in place of eggs”. She then added egg and accused me of the error.
Terrible attitude.
In addition. No toilet paper in the poorly maintained bathroom.

I’d suggest to write your dietary restrictions in a translator to avoid disappointment.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Super fresh and delicious food! 💚
Very friendly staff!

The best yellow curry I had in a while!

Must visit! 👍👍

Will be back soon! Thank you! 🙏

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Nice vegetarian restaurant in Nim Man.
Tasty, friendly staffs. Be honestly, the price is little high if compare to the same other quality foods.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
The food is decent, I enjoyed the Tom yum noodle soup and the fact that they cook with organic ingredients and have many vegan options.

The rating is for how the girl tried to shortchange me 590 baht, which is a significant amount. I handed her 1000 while I was talking to my friends and she didn’t think I would count my money and only gave me 200, when my meal cost 210. Really not cool. It was too big of a difference to know she did it on purpose.

Really disappointed when I was a return customer but now this restaurant lost 3 returning customers since my friends were appalled by that too.

Also the lime in the butterfly iced tea tasted off (too old).

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delicious vegan/vegetarian options with quaint atmosphere. Staff was super polite and the menu has English. The food itself was beautiful and very tasty, doesn't make you miss meat at all.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Pleasant restaurant with a nice atmosphere. Food was pretty good, some interrsting flavours and very fresh. It is on the pricier side though, coming in at 400 baht for three dishes and water.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: My rice was cold, soup was just chunky booked veg and tofu in water with chili flakes, fried tempeh was cold but slathered in a sweet 'tamarind' sauce. If you're a foodie or cook, try somewhere else! I said I didn't like it, but was still charged 380 Baht.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delicious food with large portions. All the food is cooked fresh from your order. Lovely quiet little place. Cool setting. Always wanted to come here. Will definitely come again.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: We decided to try this place after reading the reviews, and I'm so glad we did! We aren't vegetarians, but eat a lot of vegetarian food at home, and we really like to eat meals with lots of fresh vegetables. This place was amazing! We ate here twice in our week-long stay in Chiang Mai and the food was so flavorful and a really good value for the big portions that you get! My favorite was the pumpkin red curry and the miso eggplant. The wait for food can be long if it is crowded because there is a very small team of 2-3 in the kitchen churning out fresh food, but it is soooo worth it!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I eat spicy very often and I’ve had a lot of experience with it, but here I ordered massaman curry and it was too spicy even for me😓 They didn’t even ask about that and sure enough nothing mentioned it their menu😤 just why??? …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Not a very large selections of food. I ordered the Thai stir fry vegetables and it tasted really good. On the bottom floor they have a digital work coffee shop, and upstairs is the restaurant. I was there on a Monday afternoon and about every five minutes there was a plane boarding

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Generous portions, reasonable price, curry was so delicious.
The only thing I don't like was the Anchan tea, which was sweetened cold Butterfly pea tea, just too sweet.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This is my second time to eat in this restaurant. The food in this restaurant is great! If you're a vegetarian, the food in this restaurant will amaze you! Try it. I recommend this restaurant to all my friends！！！

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: More pricey than most Chiang Mai spots but well worth it! Just great food and drinks in general, just also happens to be vegetarian!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Don’t expect anything other than basic homemade food that you can find pretty much in any vegetarian place. Portions are big, food is spicy. They don’t have any fresh salads or fresh veggies, which was a huge minus for us.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Love this spot! It is so hard to find entire restaurants devoted to vegetarian Thai food and this spot does all the dishes very well. A lifesaver for vegetarians in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Food very good  , service and atmosphere is like eating at home. A very simple place which has lots of good energy

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
After 3 weeks in Chiang Mai, only eating vegetarian / vegan, this place is the absolute best!

Huge portions and delicious!

You can see red curry, sweet and sour stir fry, green mango salad and brown rice.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Insanely good Thai food! The massaman curry is a must try!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Tofu with black pepper and garlic was so good, as was the Anchan pad thai. This is a family owned business and they are very sweet!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: We tried to go here a few nights ago, but they were closing, though the chalkboard sign clearly said they closed at 8:30, and it was 7:45. Guess it was a slow night? We returned the next day, because I'd seen the good reviews and had a nice experience. We got the miso stirfried eggplant, and the banana flower salad. A little bit expensive, but worth it. Nice ambiance but we couldn't get the wifi to work.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Chill vibe very nice traditional Thai food green curry & fried mushrooms. Have lots of flavor and taste great. Vegetarian friendly. Green Curry is nice for a cold day. Portion is huge, so recommended to order a little and see the size first

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Wonderful little vegetarian place located upstairs, that doesn't make you miss the meat! Def recommend the corn fritters and pumpkin red curry.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Boring atmosphere but wow my green curry was so delicious perfect amount of spice  if in the neighbourhood give it a try

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Pricey for Thai food but open kitchen vegeterian Thai food. They give big portions and curries have lots of veggies and sauce. Minus a point BC Sometimes they are busy, are closed, and service is sometimes a bit lacking favourite is massamam

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: One of my favorite vegetarian spots in CM. It’s one of those places you can go back time and time again and never get tired of it! You can’t go wrong with anything on the menu, I promise!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Best mussaman curry 🤤 service is good and the food is freshly made. Vegan items are marked with an asterix and their menu changes weekly - check their Facebook for the menu. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Definitely one of the best restaurants in Chiang Mai. Huge menu, generous portions, excellent taste and cheap price. Absolutely recommended.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delicious vegetarian Thai dishes but just a little pricey compared to street options. Pleasant atmosphere in an upstairs restaurant with nice service. We had panang Curry and vegetable tempura.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Super delicious food and big portions! We recommend Fried Egg Red Curry!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
I can't believe it has taken me so long to write a review.  My first visit here I thought to myself "oh crap this is vegan food". I was not vegan and didn't have any appreciation for vegan food.

That all changed from the first bite of food.  They serve some of the best tasting meals I have ever had in my life and for only around 100 baht.  This restaurant is one of those places that make your holiday in Thailand turn into becoming an expat in Thailand.  It really does have that much of an impact because of the fresh and amazing tasting food.  I am still not a vegan, but I will eat here any chance I get because it doesn't matter.

If you are thinking about becoming healthier or vegan or need a place to take meat eaters this is it. The food is just that good

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Yummy curry options! Worth trying all of them. Also very big portions you will defenitely walk out with a full tummy.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Ate here twice and the food is delicious! Great portion sizes and probably the best stir fry I have ever had. Highly recommend visiting this gem

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Pumpkin red curry is amazing! Had it so many times and haven't gotten tired of it! It's a small restaurant and fills up quickly!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Creative and very tasty food! Last time I reviewed it was 6 years ago, and if anything it's better. Had some orange kombucha that was excellent as well.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: A great little vegetarian restaurant.  Close to Maya shopping centre but a bit off the main road.  Well worth finding. Very well priced and can take care of any dietary preferences. Would recommend getting there earlier in the day as some menu options can run out if you go near closing time.  That’s a sign of how fresh the ingredients are!  You can send a message via Facebook page (Messenger) to make a reservation and usually respond quite quickly.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delicious vegetarian restaurant with big portions! Entrees and smoothies were amazing. Staff is friendly and prices are very affordable. We’ve been back a few times and this is one of our main restaurants on our food list while staying here as a nomad.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: The staff is without smile. But here is the only place I got the hot salad. Anchan juice is just the butterfly pea juice.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: The service was good, and the Tamarind drinks were very nice. They were served in glass, which was a nice break from the plastic. The ambience is okay, and it was not too hot even though there was no aircon. However, the food was below average. We ordered a curry that looked good but had a very bland and flat taste, and it seemed to have almost no spices or herbs. It was a bit sweet and spicy, and that is really it. There were vegetables in the dishes, which was nice. The menu is written completely in english so the food is really meant for tourists.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: If you love veggies this restaurant is definitely the best one for you and a must visit whenever you're in Chiang Mai. With very reasonable price you'll have a piece of heaven.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I got the mushroom pepper garlic stir fry and it was really good. Cute Spot on the 2nd floor with balcony seating.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Very very good vegetarian and vegan food for a cheap price. We will go there again!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Good taste, not spicy at all, suitable for foreigners  But it's delicious and mellow. 👍 …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
The food was undoubtedly good for vegetarians, but the service left much to be desired. My husband was taking pictures of the restaurant for a social media review when the owner started yelling at him, telling him not to take photos. He was taking pictures in the direction of the kitchen from our table.

While it's understandable if the kitchen area is off-limits for photography, we noticed several photos of the kitchen posted by other reviewers on Google without any issue. We're not sure why my husband was singled out and scolded. It was a real disappointment and completely ruined the mood. We won't be recommending this place or returning in the future.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Good vegetarian food. Lots of vegan options as well. Huge portion sizes. Good prices. Oh btw, their banana honey coconut milk smoothie is to die for...

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: ร้านอาหารมังสวิรัติ ที่น่าทาน บรรยากาศดี อาหารอร่อย แนะนำหน่อยค่ะ

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Maybe the best red curry I can remember...every foodie should come here.
Curry was portioned perfectly, balanced flavors perfectly...and vegan too so I guess that means bonus points right?

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delicious food. Large variety and huge portion. Corn flitters must order, green curry strong taste, and if you prefer more blank do ask for less salty.  Many types of eggs and this mushroom Basil omelette is my favourite. Their butterfly …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Most definitely the BEST curry I've ever had!!! Mind blowing massaman curry and green curry.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Omnivore husband: “if vegetarian food always was this good I’d eat it all the time”. Been all over Thailand for a month, eating at all the best street food and higher end places, and this honestly was the best meal we had the whole trip. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Pretty good place! Loads of fresh veggies. Recommended !

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great food, nice service, extremely rude chef

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: If you’re in Nimman and Vegan or Vegetarian you must try Anchan. Everything on the menu is very delicious and well priced - and they have new smoothie options every day that are refreshing and addictive. Food is very taste and clean ingredients. Highly recommend!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This is a great place, I've come here for over 10 years.  I didn't give 5 stars on atmosphere simply because it has no flare. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I’m not vegetarian and this is one of my new favorite spots in Chiang Mai. I got the Pad Thai with Mushroom and Tofu and it was incredible. Generous portions and filled with veggies, noodles and the perfect seasoning. My girlfriend got the …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
Huge portions from a smart and thoughtful cook.

Even the house-specialty fried rice is well-balanced with lots of diff …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
We ate here twice and enjoyed it very much!
The food is delicious, organic and very clean-the kittchen is open.
We are not vegetarian and still it was the best and the freshest Thai food that we had during five day stay in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delicious food. Very insightful servers as well. They immediately ask if you want your meal with or without egg. Huge servings as well.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Had the sweet and sour stir fry which I had a nice tangy flavor, and the thai omelet which had too strong of a coconut oil taste. A little pricier than most vegetarian restaurants in CM. Overall pretty solid, I’ve come a few times over the years.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Super lovely place hidden on the second floor. We decided to come here because of the wide range of gluten free options. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Recently had vegetarian / vegan friends staying so tried eight restaurants in Chiang Mai that offered plant based menus that were new to me. Most were pretty good but Anchan was by far the best. As someone who is eating less meat and fish …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Masaman curry and pad Thai very good. I don’t really like pad Thai but I will eat a whole plate of theirs!! Also the banana coconut shake was amazing. They are worth all the reviews. Good yet still casual. I think the cook/ owner is quite …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Amazing dishes, freshly made while you wait. You can see them make the food. Good atmosphere. Great portion sizes and brown is included in the prices. We had the pumpkin curry and cashew nut veggies. Absolutely delicious. Paid 360b …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Delicious!!! I am Thai and have a severe seafood and nut allergy and my husband is Gluten free and this restaurant was a great find for us! I recommend the Banana Coconut Honey smoothie and the Tofu in tamarind sauce. We went 3 times in one week during our stay in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great vegetarian food. Prices are slightly above market average but still very fair. Nice dining area on the second level balcony.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Amazing butterfly pea flower soda, pad Thai and Penang curry!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great place, bookmarked it for many years and coming back to it. Food is good, price is good, service is good

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Good vegetarian food. Really big portions for Thailand. Love their smoothies too.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Excellent vegetarian place. Everything was really good. Vegetables were fresh and not oily. Curry sauce was amazing. Be warned they put egg in some dishes  unless you ask them not to.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This is my favourite restaurant in Chiang Mai! And I have been to A LOT living here now for 4 months! As a strict vegetarian I feel so comfortable eating here and knowing everything is cooked fresh and made with love. The owner is an …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This is my absolute favorite veggie place in Chiang Mai! I’m eating here almost every day and I’m always leaving full and highly satisfied. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Love this place. It is more expensive that the local street food stalls but it serves double the portion size. Food is delicious too. I had the Green Curry, which was full of flavour. Husband complained that his stir fried vegetables were tasteless though, but a few spoonfuls of soy sauce soon fixed that. Happy to visit again.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great little place. Some friends of our here suggested it and after reading the reviews we decided to try it out. We normally eat meat, but are fine with veggies only. This place was great. The textures and flavors of the food were perfect. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: เป็นอาหารมังสวิรัติ มีไข่ มีหอม แต่ว่าแจ้งทางร้านได้ว่าไม่เอา รสชาติไม่จืด แต่ก็ไม่เข้มมาก ต่างชาติทานได้

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
On the 2nd floor, half open, cozy restautant
Tempura murshroom salad surprised me, papaya lime smoothie is also very nice

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This place is so good! We had cashew tofu stir fry and pad Thai and both agreed it was some of the best food we had in Thailand. Also reasonably prices (especially for the area) and big servings. Highly recommend.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: My fiance and I are not vegetarians but decided to try this place as we had heard good things about it. The seating is very limited and we were a little confused on finding the place which is upstairs. The food was excellent and the drinks …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: One of the best vegan food in Chiang Mai town with big serving and reasonable price.  One of the best green curry I have ever tried during my months of stay in Thailand. Please don't be greedy,  their food serving are quite big. Delicious food and enjoy everyone... .

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Amazing!! Really delicious food! A little more pricey than average but you get a big portion plus it all comes with rice so it turns out the same. Highly recommended :) So happy to have so many great veg options in Chiang Mai :)

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Tasty food with fresh high organic vegetables but at a higher price than usual. But worth it!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Really tasty vegan food in a home-y setting! To note: you can only pay by cash💶 …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I've been to this shop for over five years. I can. Say It's super consistent! Highly recommended! My favorite dishes are mixed veggies & massaman curry. I can say it's in my top 5 restaurants in Chiang Mai 🙏🫡 …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Wow! We stopped here for dinner tonight after reading the glowing reviews and understand why this place is so loved! The restaurant itself is nicely decorated and clean with an open terrace. The staff members were so friendly! We ordered …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Absolute favorite veggie restaurant in Chiang Mai. I’m a meat eater and I find dishes here 100% delicious. The flavors are rich, portion sizes are generous and even better, without fish sauce (I honestly couldn’t tell). We went two nights …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Absolutely amazing and healthy food! I cannot stress how good the Massaman Curry dish is - easily in my top three favourite dishes of all time (my partner agrees). Perfectly flavoured, might be a tad on the spicy side for wimps like us, but …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: We've ordered green curry just about every day we've been in Thailand (approx. 24 days in 5 different provinces) and this is our favorite. We ordered that, the morning glory stir fry, and the eggplant ragu macaroni. It was hard to pick …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: My favourite vegetarian spot in Chiang Mai! The owners are very kind and the food is always delicious. Big quantities! Enough to share or take some for leftovers. My favourite dishes are the massaman curry and red pumpkin curry.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I had the banana blossom salad and it was delish (and a bonus to not have fish sauce in a dish in Thailand) and a large portion!  I also had a banana, coconut honey smoothie and it too was delish, not too sweet, but just right!  I ordered a …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Best Thai food we had in Thailand! The smoothies were amazing, the noodles were perfect!! Thai basil rice was my favorite, it was a little spicy but amazing overall!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: So I'm not sure what happened with some of the really poor ratings below, but for me the quality of the food here was excellent, with generous portion size. I had the banana flower salad, corn fritters and cinnamon + ginger drink. The …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: One of the best Vegan/Veg places I've been to in Thailand. We ordered quite a few dishes and they were all amazing! Best Pad Thai I've had in Thailand so far - totally recommend trying it if you decide on visiting.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Very good vegan vegetarian fare, prices are average for the area. We had the Pad Thai Anchan and the stir-fried garlic pepper tofu both were delicious but if I had to pick a winner  it would be the Pad Thai Anchan. Would definitely return if I found myself in Chiang Mai again.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Amazing vegetarian food from uncle anchan. Really not easy to have just one cook  cooking everything and serving it up really quickly. If you want some homely thai style food, do visit anchan. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Food was so freaking good!! Had the Panang curry, pad Thai, fried tofu, and papaya salad and every dish was the best we’ve had since being in Chiang Mai. My daughter had the mango smoothie which she has had every where we’ve been and she said this one was far and above the others. Delicious vegan food!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great food. I'm not vegetarian but have been here three times and always enjoyed the food. Prices are a little on the high side especially considering there's no meat which you'd think would bring prices down. On the other hand, serving …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: This place has very friendly staff, incredible food, & VERY tasty smoothies all at good prices! Would highly recommend :)

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
One of favorite vegetarian restaurant in nimman area.
Good price , good healthy Thai style foods.
Drink also good here too. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Pricey but usually good food. 1 time (out of many) my friend found some ants in her curry, obviously they made their excuses and didn't charge it on the bill. Ants taste fine by the way, but they are not vegetarian. ;) …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: They usually change up the menu a little bit, the food has great flavor, although sometimes I think a bit sweet on some dishes. They use a nice brown rice combination. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: One of the best vegetarian meals I've had in Thailand so far! Prices are fair considering the size of the portions (80-120 baht per dish) and that rice is included. Highly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: We were two and ordered the Pad Thai, Indian curry and papaya salad. The Indian curry was by far the most flavorful and delicious and in general we felt like dining in heaven. Great place for a higher quality food and slightly higher prices (100-150bht)

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Absolutely wonderful food and so clean and healthy! Spend four months in Chiang Mai and would visit this place at least once a week. My favourites are massaman and indian yellow curry and was never disappointed!

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: I really can't understand the hype for this restaurant. It's just one of all those places who sacrificed the authentic Thai flavor for a pale, tasteless variant adapted to the tourists' taste buds. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Really great and delicious food for vegetarians. Organic ingredients, and best of all… NO MSG, which is really difficult to find in Thailand in traditional Thai food places.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great Vegetarian restaurant. I'm an omnivore, but the vegetarian dish that I had at Anchan was really, really good and packed with vegetables. It was the tofu fried rice with mixed vegetables, highly recommended. My wife had the veggie Thai omelet, extra tasty. Our whole bill came out to 310 BHT.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Yummy Thai Vegan, good price.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: If you are looking for vegetarian friendly and authentic thai food than this is place to be. We ordered (Anchan / purple colored ) Pad Thai, fried rice and masaman curry, all three tasted great and felt different in a good way from American Thai restaurants.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Good quality food, but a little more expensive than the best value vegetarian restaurants in Chiang Mai. Relaxing attractive place overlooking quiet street. But didn't feel feel fully comfortable with the young staff, who typically seem mainly interested in themselves.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great.  I was impressed and will return.   The restaurant was upstairs with an open air view over the street. …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Some of the best Thai fushion vegetarian food you will find in Chiang Mai. Their specialties are cooked however, so if you are seeking crunchy freshness go elsewhere. This place is all about well-balanced flavours in green curries, Asian stir fries and moreish tofu dishes. A little pricey but well worth it.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Anchan is a winner.  A very vegan friendly restaurant with a great menu and fantastic food.  We had Panang Curry and Massaman Curry on two different nights -- both were very good.  A bit expensive by local standards but a steal by western …

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: i had a decent masaman curry and a pretty good banana coconut milk smoothie. it would have been a better experience if the owner had been at least a little happy to have me as a customer. he seemed like he wasn't all that interested in having customer. the wife? owner was at least somewhat pleasant.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Dont be misled by size or location of the restaurant. Yummiest veggie food we ever had..tamarind tea and smoothie was delicious..too bad we couldn't visit again because we were on a short trip to Chiang Mai. Highly recommend.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text:
The fried rice I had was the best I had so far in Thailand.
Very big dishes.
Great place :)

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: The best thai food I've ever had. I've lived in Thailand for a year now and Anchan is by far the best. Its not too greasy, they put loads of veggies in and every dish I've ordered has been exceptional.

Restaurant_Name: Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant
Review_Text: Great little find! The food was great! I had the pad Thai and it had so many hearty veggies in it. The best I have had so far. I am a meat eater but used to be vegan and I love how well portioned, priced and tastey this place is. Thanks to whoever blogged about this because I never in a million years would have found it! 😊 …

Restaurant_Name: yesterday cafe'
Review_Text:
Something magical about this place because I was mysteriously pulled in while walking past the cafe. ❤️

I knew it was going to be good as soon as I saw the design, the architecture, the old wood, and the coffee bar inside. I was immediately greeted by the manager who was very helpful and informative. I don't usually choose coffee bars, but this one is special. After 5 minutes of learning about their slow drip process and the different coffees they offered, I chose the Ethiopian bean.

They take their time and bring you what you need to enjoy the coffee. The Ethiopian blend was amazing. Exactly as described. I drank it without any sugar or cream. I had to have the chocolate croissant.

The seating is comfortable. The atmosphere is relaxing and great for meetings, couples, or by yourself. You don't hear a lot of street noise if you sit inside. I can tell that it would be fun to sit outside in the evenings when it's cooler and you can watch people walk by.

I definitely found a great cafe and will come back. I highly recommend this place if you are picky about coffee and want to experience something different than your average coffee shop. The cost is worth the visit. You are paying for quality and ambience as well as for great service.

Restaurant_Name: yesterday cafe'
Review_Text:
A Disastrous Start to 2026 at Yesterday Cafe
Rating: ★☆☆☆☆
Date of Visit: January 1, 2026 | 8:00 AM
If you find yourself in Chiang Mai, a city globally renowned for its world-class coffee culture and unparalleled hospitality, do yourself a favor: keep walking. There are arguably a thousand cafes in this city—many of them hidden gems tucked away in narrow sois—that offer soul-stirring brews and genuine smiles. Unfortunately, Yesterday Cafe is not one of them. My visit at 8:00 AM on New Year’s Day was a masterclass in how to alienate a customer within ten minutes of opening the doors.
The Service: Cold and Uncooperative
Walking in on the first morning of the year, one might expect a modicum of holiday cheer or, at the very least, basic professional courtesy. Instead, I was met by a man and a woman behind the counter whose collective energy could best be described as "aggressively indifferent."
The primary reason I stepped foot into Yesterday Cafe was to utilize their signature delayed delivery postcard service. It’s a charming concept—writing a letter to your future self or a loved one to be delivered months or years down the line. However, a "mail-themed" cafe that doesn't understand the mechanics of mailing is like a library that doesn't believe in books.
When I inquired about purchasing stamps to actually send the postcard, the interaction turned into a frustrating exercise in "the run-around." Rather than providing a straightforward answer or helping me complete the process, the staff were unhelpful, vague, and remarkably non-accommodating. It felt as though my request for a stamp—a fundamental component of their entire business hook—was a personal grievance to them. If you are going to brand your entire aesthetic around "delayed mail," you should probably ensure your staff knows how a mailbox works.
The Product: Overpriced and Underwhelming
Then, there is the matter of the coffee. In a city where you can get an artisanal, locally-sourced bean prepared by a champion barista for a fair price, paying 89 Baht for a plain Americano is a steep ask. For that price, I expected a balanced, flavorful cup.
What I received was, quite frankly, terrible. The coffee "sucked" in the most literal sense—it was thin, over-extracted, and lacked any of the complex notes one expects from Chiang Mai’s coffee scene. It tasted like an afterthought. To charge premium prices for sub-par swill while providing bottom-tier service is a bold strategy, but not one that earns repeat customers.
Final Verdict: Don't Waste Your Time
The irony of the name "Yesterday Cafe" isn't lost on me; after five minutes inside, I certainly wished I was back in yesterday, so I could choose a different place to spend my money. It is rare to find a business that fails so spectacularly on both its core product (the coffee) and its unique selling point (the mail service).
The "delayed delivery" gimmick is the only thing this place has going for it, and they couldn’t even get that right. Save your 89 Baht, save your morning, and go literally anywhere else. In a city of a thousand cafes, this one deserves to be at the very bottom of your list.
