Bone marrow is nice with the beef tartare served at the side.

Restaurant_Name: Fresh Harvest Farm Chiang Mai
Review_Text:
I recently visited Fresh Harvest Farm Steakhouse at 6/25 Nimman Soi 2 in Chiang Mai, and it was an absolutely fantastic experience. From the moment I walked in, the service was personable and welcoming. The staff made sure to attend to every detail, enhancing the overall dining experience.

The food was exceptional. I tried the Thai Beef with bone marrow, and it was an absolute treat. The presentation was beautiful, and the flavors were spot on—rich, savory, and perfectly balanced with Thai herbs. The steak was cooked to perfection, tender and flavorful, accompanied by a delightful assortment of grilled vegetables. The bone marrow with Thai herbs was another standout, a delicious and indulgent dish that I highly recommend.

Additionally, the draft beer selection was impressive, providing the perfect pairing for the rich and hearty dishes. The atmosphere of the restaurant is both relaxed and inviting, making it a must-visit location when in Chiang Mai. Whether you're a local or a traveler, Fresh Harvest Farm offers a memorable dining experience that should not be missed. If you're ever in Chiang Mai, make sure to stop by and experience it for yourself.

Christopher
Adventures of Stix-Dean

Restaurant_Name: Fresh Harvest Farm Chiang Mai
Review_Text:
Great restaurant serving a wide variety of delicious food with the restaurant being famous for its farm diect Wagyu beef. The restaurant farms its own beef to supply this and its three other branches in Bangkok.
There is a very large selection of various cuts and weights of steak on display in their fridges. Simply select your preferred cut and choose your sides and they will cook it to your preferences in the kitchen.
Please allow time for the steak to be excellency prepared while you enjoy a drink from their huge selection of craft or draught beers and ciders. Good wine selection also available by the glass or bottle.
I found that the food was both very good and excellent value for money and look forward to a return visit to sample more.
The manager here is a real professional and extremely knowledgeable on beef.

Service was prompt and pleasant.

If you want bedding this is definitely worth a try.

We ordered a set which is for 4 pax consist of green curry, friend shrimp, a whole fish, salad with beef, fried noodle style appetizer and fruit platter. On top, we ordered a unique unpasteurised Chang beer which is 1.5 litres like a champagne bottle. Food served very quick and the servers keep ensuring our drink cups is always full. Shop wise is very unique like a antique shop with a few types of grandfather clock. Overall, it is cheap and food is fantastic. Really recommend for those who wants local food over here.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The food was amazing here. Super reasonably priced compared to other Thai places in the area and very homely. We got the Isan-style larb moo and we really loved it. The Pad See Ew and Pad Thai were amazing too and they accommodated for our spicy levels. One of the best Thai restaurants in the area that’s reasonably priced and very homely ❤️

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ร้านอาหารห้องแถว เป็นอาหารไทย ทีีได้มิชิลิน เมนู หลนปลาสลิต และแกงคั่วหอยขม เมนูอื่นๆ อร่อยแบบดั้งเดิม บริการดี ร้านจะเก่าๆตามบรรยากาศ

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Very good traditional Thai restaurant. Every cuisine looks interesting and I can’t imagine I ordered 4 dishes and all finished by myself. The restaurant is nice and cozy. The service of an old lady is great(she might be the owner of the restaurant). Very nice and with good smile. I really like the fried rice. So delicious and not spicy at all.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
Decent flavor on the Khao Soi, but it was served with pieces of chicken breast instead of club. It was a little strange, but the taste was okay overall. The consistency of the crispy noodles were also a bit off.

We ordered 2 of the khao soi chicken, og one of us got more chicken than the other.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: This Thai restaurant offers a delightful experience with its charming, old-fashioned atmosphere. While the food was quite enjoyable, the portions might be considered somewhat modest. The staff are welcoming, and the ambiance is very homey. It is certainly worth recommending to both tourists and local residents.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: It was very valued for money as the dishes I had and a bottle of water cost me 240b , nevertheless it was open till 10pm if you gonna have a late dinner after a outskirt day's trip or etc . I ordered the fried small shrimp biscult-thingy and grass jelly based on previous review. It was decent but felt if there's spicy chilli to dip with , it would be great based on my taste . The grass jelly was pretty good too .

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The restaurant is quite unique, it feels like an Asian colonial era hotel lobby, with a large collection of old grandfather clocks. We visited twice and the food was consistently good both times.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: I always come here for brunch or dinner, gotta say that they did an amazing job with their foods, I absolutely loved whatever they cooked for me, I had tried many dishes and all are stunning, would pretty much recommend anyone who loves Thai cuisine to give them a chance to show you how authentic it is here, also aunties and uncles here are super lovely!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: We just wanted non-spicy and fuss free fare, so selected 4 simple dishes at 600B (with drinks) for 3 pax. Loved how each dish was executed, especially the fried tofu with peanut dip! The eggs with tamarind sauce turned out better than expected. The stir fried chicken with cashew nuts (didn't take pic) was not bad too. Liked the unassuming service, home style but tasty food, and the classic retro ambience. Would love to return to try more dishes.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The ambiance is homey and welcoming. We had the khao rad nah kai and the khao soi. Both are really delicious. The staff are also quite nice.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: เป็นร้านเก่าแก่ เป็นร้านตำนาน ร้านหนึ่งในเชียงใหม่ เลยก้อว่าได้  มาครั้งนี้  สั่ง ข้าวน้ำพริกชงเรือ , แกงเขียวหวานปลาดุก, หมี่กรอบ, กุ้งฝอยทอด, ก๋วยเตี๋ยวหลอดทรงเครื่อง  รู้สึกผิดหวัง กับรสชาติ และ คุณภาพของอาหาร ด้อยลงกว่าเดิม  หรือว่ามาทาน ตอน 15:30 นะ. แม่ครัวพีอครีวัวจริงยังไม่มา

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Looks like this place has been in business for a long time.  And I think there's a reason why.  It's probably quite consistent with making food to foreign palettes.  I'm from Taiwan and I think it is more the style for visitors.  It still tastes good, no doubt.  The portions for each dish is a little small. The restaurant is filled with visitors and I'm glad they had a table for 9.  I recommend this restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The place has an old, nostalgic feel that’s actually quite relaxing. I tried their Khao Soi, a Chiang Mai specialty. It wasn’t spicy and very easy to eat.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Food was great, easy to order and satisfying. Thai tea was old style, and pretty good. Grass jelly was the actual good grass jelly, with a good chew, but could have a little more ice.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
Very beautiful and nice place. The staff is amazing and the venue is extremely charming. They have a really impressive grandfather clock right at the entrance.

Honestly it looks like a Studio Ghibli interior, however the food we had was safe to say underwhelming.

We ordered whole  chicken but I wouldn’t recommend it to you. The chicken is deep fried and extremely dry and crunchy. Hard to eat and it feels like there is not a lot of meat left.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The restaurant has got lots of decorations from decades ago, beautiful and cozy setting.  The food is also amazing, although I'm not local, I do feel that the flavors in this restaurant is much less "for foreigners" and closer to the ones I had together with other Thai friends.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Absolutely delicious!!!   Best feed i have had in Thailand.  Set 2 (for two people) was brilliant and we had two other dishes to make it for three, but actually one more would have been sufficient. So impressed we have been back since and planning on lunch tomorrow too!  Well worth a visit.   The fermented pork dish was bloody amazing and the red curry the best I have ever eaten.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
I can see why people come here: lots of indoor seating, ample air conditioning, and the place looks clean and hygienic.

Ordered a pad kra pao moo and a some flat noodles in tom yum soup.

The food wasn’t bad was wasn’t very good either: quite average.

You’re also paying 100 to 150 baht for dishes that would cost 60 to 100 at other places.

I won’t be coming back here as there are so many other restaurants here in Chiangmai that have very high ratings and tasty Thai fare.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
I ate alone at noon and went to a northern Thai restaurant called Hong Tauw Inn. I thought it was a tavern or a homestay just by looking at the word "Inn".
There are so many dishes that I have a little difficulty choosing. For example, soups include minced meat tofu soup, minced meat Chinese cabbage soup, pineapple sliced ​​meat soup, Tom Yum Kung soup, etc., as well as curry and various. I chose a soft shell crab, northern Thai sausage and water spinach. To be honest, the dishes are not big, and the dishes in another northern Thai restaurant that I went with my relatives and friends before were not big.
Then I ordered a cup of iced coffee and forgot to remind me not to add sugar, but it was still delicious.
The atmosphere in the store is very impressive. After eating, it was raining a lot when I went out. At that time, I regretted that I should have stayed for a while after eating, and I didn’t take a few more photos of the environment.
The price of the dishes is a bit high for Thailand, nearly 100 yuan Chinese yuan for one person, but the taste and environment are also worthy of the price. There is an aunt inside, and the service is very meticulous.
During the meal, there was a girl by the window by the side who propped up her mobile phone on a small tripod and kept recording videos, presumably she was a domestic self-media blogger.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
We found this restaurant just when we decided not to go to the initial place we intended to. No regrets. The service was really nice; I even asked for their help to charge my phone. The food, especially, was really good and tasty.

It was our last night in Chiang Mai, and we were happy to have our dinner here. My favorite dish is pad krapow, and they have a beef version with the perfect amount of spiciness and delicious flavor. I love the food so much that I need to leave them a review!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: เป็นอาหารที่อร่อยมาก คนต่างชาติทานก็ว่าอร่อย คนไทยทานก็ชอบ นึกถึงอาหารไทยทานง่ายๆ คือร้านนี้เลย รสชาติอร่อยทุกเมนู ร้านดูร่วมสมัยมากด้วย

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
Came here twice. My favorites are papaya salad, chicken fried rice and fried prawns. The papaya salad is bursting with flavors, sour, sweet, spicy, just perfect. The prawns are very fresh, crispy on the outside and moist in the inside.

The pictures below are from my 2nd visit, mango salad, fried prawns and crab fried rice.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: One of my favorite restaurants in Chiang Mai - a definite must visit when I visit again. We were so lucky to stay near this restaurant and chance upon it. I love the homey cosy vibe and the food is so amazing. We loved every single dish and the ginger juice is out of this world. The ginger juice is amazing and great for sore throat and stomach unrest. The fried rice was consistently good - pork, crab and the best version is salted fish. The salted egg morning glory is very delicious too. We also really enjoyed the Tom Ka coconut soup - very umami even though we asked for no spice. The fried fish, fried tofu were also really delicious. A must get is the unassuming dessert - glutinous rice balls and ginger soup. The roasted black sesame filling is so good and the glutinous rice skin was so light and chewy. All went perfectly with the tasty ginger soup. A great gem of the Nimman area. Very reasonably priced and the food is amazing!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: We’ve visited here twice, and all the food has been great! We especially love the 302 Kuay Teow Tom Yum Koong—it’s the best.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Came back after a few years and the food is still very good as with the previous time. A great place to have authentic Thai dishes at very good prices. The very little small shrimps as the menu calls it is nicely and thinly fried - crunchy yumminess!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
Locals recommended us this Thai restaurant. And granted, it was amazing.

It combines traditional flavours with a unique interior design. The staff are understanding and helpful. The portions are good for sharing and very much worth it for the price.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Northern Thai cuisine restaurant with an atmosphere like dining at a friend’s home. Moderate pricing, around 500 baht for two people. The staff provides good service, and the food is delicious. Located opposite One Nimman.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Food taste pretty good, but the portion is smaller than expected. Not sure about the hygiene because few hours after the lunch, both my wife and me have stomachache.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
The food is good very nice Thai dish that can't find everywhere they serving here.

We order the salted egg and shrimp paste very nice creamy and slicly spicy.. also the young coconut stir fry with shrimp is very nice sauce with little garlic. So good will come back again for sure.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Many choices of Northern Foods are reserved for you here. It is crowded on my visiting day, so it causes a long time of cooking. If you do not like sweetness, you should tell staff. The restaurant atmosphere is like house or old house style.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Rustic decor and delicious food. Once you set foot into the restaurant it takes you back to the 1930s. Everything looks as though it stood still in time if you like the antique feel this is the place to be. Food was delicious and simply presented, it actually looks like your grandmothers cooking and presentation. Everything was cooked to perfection and it is extremely flavourful. Something to take noted if you are in a rush you may want yo make time because each dish will take some time to be served.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Very lively family owned restaurant. We ordered papaya salad, tom yum kung, fried tilapia and sweet vegetable. The tom yum kung was rich and had good amount of ingredients in it. They have already filtered out the lemongrass and leaves from the soup which is nice. The papaya salad was scrumptious and have just enough spice in it. I loved the fish and the sauce. It was not oily and very crispy. Nicely done. Would return

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The Hong Tauw Inn is one of my favorite Thai restaurants in Chiang Mai. Red curry with pork was flavorful and pomelo salad was so good. Narm Phrig Koog Pow was my favorite menu. Highly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Tasted fine, but the duck one has almost no good meat in it.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: อาหารจานไม่ใหญ่ สามารถสั่งได้หลายอย่าง อร่อยทุกอย่าง แกงคั่วหอยขมเข้มข้นมาก บรรยากาศเหมือนนั่งกินข้าวอยู่บ้าน ร้านมีที่จอดรถอยู่ด้านหลัง

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
A hidden gem. Everything from the food, the price, the menu selection, the atmosphere and the service were incredible.

Lon tau jiaw, a northern bean-paste dish with minced pork, was my favourite. The menu is large and there's a lot of different choices. Everything we ordered was above average.

There wasn't any queue when we arrived and it looks quite unassuming from the outside.

I find the numerous clocks and the occasional chimes to be a unique touch which differentiates it from a regular cafe/restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: No frills restaurant serving authentic Thai cuisine. Highly recommend the Tom Kha Pla (coconut curry with fish) - extremely flavorful and fresh - easily the best I've had! Location is great as well - just across the street from One Nimman.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
the place is located in Nimman road(near One Nimman soi)....it's a very cosy place(look like you are in Thai house for eat).....we had some small "problem" but except from this problem everything was great!!
i begin with the "problem":we went in the place (in the morning)and we book a table for our dinner in the night....we book for 4 people and we go there at 8.00 pm
we arrined there in 7.55 pm and we saw that some other people was sitting in our table!
when we ask why is that happening,they told us:"do not  worry...they leaving in 5 minutes...they already ask for the bill"....and theuy offer us another table (so we do not stand) until they other people leave....to tell you the truth,i got mad about this...the other company left at 8.15 pm...so we waited in another table 15 minutes....after they leave they clean up the table and we transfer there
i know some people say that is not important but what is the meaning of booking  a table???it's not to wait !!!
anyway....except that incident everything was great...the food very good(this is a Thai reaturant...so no western food)...the decor also nice(cosy)....and the prices very good(i could say almost cheap)....,,very good service from the staff also.....
the place is closing 10.30 pm(if i am not mistaken)...so you betetr go early to eat your Thai Food
TIP:I tried the new Chang Beer Champagne(it is beer in a bottle of Champagne....the staff told us that is made the same way the make champagne...and of course more expensive than the normal beer)
Guys....it was the same taste like normal Chang Beer!!!!so...you do not get fooled and order this!!!order normal Chang beer better!!!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The food is very tasty specially the Pad Thai, one of the best I had tried in Chiang Mai but I don’t think I’ll revisit since it’s abit more pricier then the usual pad Thai and since it’s near a tourist spot so it’s understandable. And other reason is that the all the dish is very small in portion, even the drinks is so much smaller…..

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Good tasting food! Each dish served in small portion so you can order many variety of menu. We ordered 11 dishes and 3 large beer for about 1,700 baht.  The service us very friendly but can be a bit slow.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
ร้านน่ารักมาก มีความclassic และ homey มากๆ อาหารรสชาติเหมือนกินข้าวรสมือแม่ และราคาไม่แพงด้วย

ชอบปลาช่อนห้องแถว น้ำพริกไข่เค็ม และแกงเผ็ดเป็ดย่าง

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ร้านอาหารไทย ที่อยู่นิมมานเหมินท์​ ร้านหาได้ไม่ยาก อยู่ติดถนน ใกล้ๆ Think Park. อาหารรสชาติถูกปาก ถ้ามาช่วงลูกค้าเยอะ อาจจะต้องรอสักหน่อย เนื่องจากร้านได้ทำทีละจาน บรรยากาศ​ดี ได้อารมณ์​เหมือนนั่งทานข้าวที่บ้าน #ลงพุง​คอนเฟิร์ม​

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ร้านตกแต่งสไตล์แนวของเก่า รสชาติอาหารดีเลยล่ะ อาหารจานไม่ใหญ่แต่อร่อยทุกจาน น้ำพริกอร่อยมากๆ แกงเผ็ดเป็ดย่างก็ดี มีโอกาสจะไปซ้ำอีกแน่ๆ มีที่จอดรถด้านหลังนะ

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: First of all, for the food quality and dining environment vs price, definitely worth a 5 stars.  Tastewise, I will give it a 7.7 out 10 of my own scale (based on the foods I ordered). Again, I think it serves quality local Tai food.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ร้านอาหารไทยตรงข้ามโครงการ One Nimman รสชาติดี แต่ออกไปทางหวาน จืด อาจจะถูกปากกับชาวต่างชาติ แต่บรรยากาศในร้านดีมากๆ ครับ เงียบสงบ เหมาะสำหรับพาครอบครัวมาทาน

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Discovered Hong Tauw Inn after searching for Khao Soi and decided to check them out. Upon entering, I felt like going back in time! I was warmly greeted and shown to my seat and ordered Khao Soi and fried bean curd on the first night. Food was delivered promptly and taste exceed my expectations. Everything was authentic and delicious so much so I returned on the second night for fried rice and shrimp. Just a heads up, food is a little hot if you can’t hold your spicy level and that’s what made the food amazing. Highly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
5’s for what it is! A rare busy tourist-and-local welcoming place serving up affordable ‘real’ if foreigner- and family-friendly food with proficiency and familiarity in a clean-enough, climate controlled restaurant setting one of the busiest commercial districts anywhere.

On food - it feels like going to a distant uncle’s house for a huge family gathering, where uncle isn’t necessarily a foodie but he likes his food the way it’s always been, not distinguished or extreme in any way (whether freshness or presentation or decor), in fact anti-dazzling, just absolutely culturally correct.

On service - I went at 7:30 pm and they were super busy! The hosts and servers were running about; so many tables with understaffing that they covered well for; seemed tired with the rigmarole (staff retreats and extended vacations not a thing here), but on it and as responsive as they can be. I had to wait to add something to the menu, and wave a few people down for the check. No prob!

On ambiance - did I say uncle’s house? Comfy, worn, funny mix of faux thai artifacts, irregularly hung no working clocks, birds and random auntie-painted watercolors in plastic frames, dated tables with clean cloths, dim lit fluorescent lights, friendly jovial happy family talking sounds in Japanese, Korean, Thai all around.

Explains the high fives! A familiar, comfortable, warm, worn and welcoming place in the middle of pizazz and bustle. Like an old Italian joint in Times Square…

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: A Thai friend recommended this Thai restaurant in the busy Ninma Road. Setting gets nostalgic feel. Dishes are authentic.  If you like to taste special tradition dish, try nam phrig, a mixture mainly made with dried shrimp paste. Service staff very nice.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ร้านเก่าแก่บนถนนนิมมาน เหมาะกับผู้ชื่นชอบอาหารไทย รสชาติดี ราคาไม่แรงจนเกินไป ไปอีกได้เรื่อยๆ นะ

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Nice cozy little restaurant with cheap but yummy food. The way their tom yum noodle tasted was different from the ones i've had before. More on the sour side but it was definitely my favourite! Cash only unless u spend over 500 baht, and even for two people, the bill only came out to be under 400 baht.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Didn’t have good experiences, we went there pretty late like almost 9pm the restaurant wasn’t busy. I watched most ppl already got food and chill. We ordered padkeemao, fish cake and green curry. We had to waited for 45min to get the first dish and another 10min for my green curry. I guess the server forgot to put my order. The youngest server seems didn’t know what is she doing, looks confused and served us warm beer. The food wasn’t good either. Bland and the fish cake was chewy. Disappointed

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
Went to this restaurant after reading the positive reviews on TripAdvisor. We had lunch and dinner on 2 separate occasions. Lunch was rather disappointing. Ordered the pineapple fried rice and it tasted rather bland, devoid of stir-fried flavor which you would expect from this famous dish. Dinner was slightly better. We had the stir-fry flat rice noodles (kway teow), crab meat fried rice, appetizer and desserts. The main dishes were palatable but I can't say the same for the dessert. While most dishes are inexpensive, do note that the portions are rather small.

Restaurant is conveniently located on the main street of Nimmanhaemin, close to Maya mall and U Nimman Hotel. The food is pretty much average, nothing to shout about. So go only if you ran out of dining options.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Cosy restaurant serving authentic Thai food......tried their spicy sausage...fried chicken... pineapple fried rice....pomelo salad ...fried tofu

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: A simple delicious Thai dishes!  This is my third time dining here and the food were delicately prepared and tasted authentic Thai dishes very time.  They also received a recognition from a well known organization for good food, good taste as well. Glad to come back

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
รสชาติอาหารค่อนข้างโอเคเลย​ ที่ชอบจะเป็นเนื้อย่างน้ำจิ้มแจ๋ว​หอม​ น้ำพริกหนุ่มรสจัดเผ็ดแต่อร่อย​ ของทอดอร่อยทุกอย่างแต่นี่สั่งทั้งเต้าหู้ทอดและกุ้งแพทอด​ หมูคำหวานรสชาติดี

พนักงานบริการดี
บรรยากาศร้านถ้าชอบแนววินเทจออกแนวค่อนข้างเก่านิดนึงน่าจะชอบ​ ร้านติดมดกระจกเยอะ​ เข้าห้องน้ำแล้วตกใจ​😅

รวมๆค่อนข้างโอเค​

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: อาหารไทยอร่อย รสชาดดี บรรยากาศดี ชอยการตกแต่ง มีนาฬิกาเยอะมากกกก พนักงานน่ารัก ดูแลดีค่ะ

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: We loved this place, we ate there 3 times, each time we had 4 different dishes, all delicious and full of flavor.  The restaurant is like a little house, filled with antiques.  Inexpensive!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ร้านห้องแถวที่อยู่ในห้องแถว แต่รสชาติไม่ธรรมดา อร่อยยันข้าว ข้าวนุ่มอร่อยจนเผลอกินไปสองจาน! อาหารส่วนใหญ่เป็นอาหารภาคกลาง มีอาหารเหนือนิดหน่อย สั่งน้ำพริกมะขามมารสชาติกลมกล่อมดีสุดๆ  เสิร์ฟมาพร้อมกับผักสด หมูหวาน ปลากรอบ ไข่เค็ม ข้าวหมดจานในพริบตา แกงฮังเลก็ดี ติดหวานไปนิด โดยรวมๆ เป็นอีกร้านที่มองข้าม แต่วันนี้ระหว่างรอห้องที่รร.อีสติน ตัน ขี้เกียจไปไกล แล้วพนักงานรร.แนะนำร้านนี้มา ไม่ผิดหวัง ร้านด้านหน้าดูเล็กแต่ด้านในโต๊ะเยอะมาก เป็นอีกร้านที่แนะนำ

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
I ordered a full fried chicken and a watermelon drink.

The chicken was very dry and it didn't have a lot of meat on the bone. I had a hard time eating it since it wasn't a lot to work with.

The watermelon juice was okay, but just okay.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Delicious food and great service. The restaurant has a very nice atmosphere and offers great choice of Northern Thai dishes. The prices are good and food is excellent.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Was really good. We ordered the khao soy which had no chicken bone as they usually do, which is a plus, and the papaya salad and it was delicious.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The first time weate here was 15 years ago when a friend Ade brought us here when our daughter attended boarding school in Chiang Mai.  Today we tried many items from the menu and found everything to be absokutely delicious. All ingredients were fresh  and savoury. The Ka Nom Pang Nah Moo with its dip made my taste buds do back flips, the Tao Hu Thod with sweet n sour sauce kept my taste buds in heaven. Next was the Yum Som O pomollo salad with kanom jeen noodles. The sauce accented the flavor and allowed you to taste everything fully. Last was our khao Soi Gai with a broth so tastey  i wanted to lick the bowl. Closing with our favorite desert Bualoy Nga Dum Namking.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Beautiful restaurants, serving very tasty Thai dishes!! We loved our food and would highly recommend!!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
What an authentic Thai food and yes I’m Thai!!!.
Just across the road from hectic One nimman.
Staff and service are great.
LOVE IT!! Please try.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: A place to enjoy Thai food in a clean restaurant. Good fried shrimp, friendly staff and clean restrooms.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Great restaurant with Thai cuisine. Cozy indoor setting. The pad kra pow was delicious and spicy. Great friendly service too.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Well... I've been in Thailand over 2 months now and this was my best meal so far.  I had Nam Prik Ong (tomato, pork and chilli dip) and Gaeng Hung Lay (pork and ginger curry) and both dishes were phonomonal.  I've eaten both dishes before and also cooked them myself at home but these were the best I've ever had.  Will definitely be back while I'm visiting Chiang Mai and recommended this restaurant to anyone.  Delicious  !!!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Very cozy Thai family style restaurant. We really enjoyed the food here. Be aware that some dishes are quite spicy. Love the mango sticky rice here!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: When I walk around nearby at the dinner time, I have a good feeling with this place, so I walk in to order this green curry shrimp and  coconut.  Its a local taste.  Its spicy, but really good!  And the staff are nice, very nice price.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Excellent Northern Thai menu with some westernised favourites, if you don't want hit they will happily make your food less spicy. Excellent value and very old world feel.. Lots of antique looking clocks 🤔 …

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
My most favorite restaurant in Chiang Mai! I loved the cozy and friendly atmosphere of the restaurant. And all the foods were delicious. Their Khao Soi noodles is probably the best among many others that I tried in Chiang Mai. And also the other items, such as Spring Rolls, Vegetables, Salads everything was fantastic. And price of the foods was pocket friendly, too! Definitely visiting this gem every time I go to Chiang Mai.

Update: Yes, I visited again in 2023. And obviously will visit again if I get the chance to go to CM again.

Update 2: Yea, visited again in 2025. Everything still delicious!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Authentic Thai food with really great true delicious taste. Operated by a friendly family and the place has a traditional classic dining atmosphere. There're elderly gentleman and lady taking care of the customers. As close to a home food as you can get.  For me, the best place for Khao Soi, among others. Been coming here every time I come to Chiang Mai. The place is quiet nowadays since the Covid times began, but I'm glad they are still catering to those who know this place, mostly due to their family-owned restaurant and own building, I suppose. The food tastes remain the same since I first started coming here.

Review_Text: If you are mid age Asian, you will love this atmosphere. This place brings back a lot of childhood memories. The fried kuey teow (pad Thai) is a must! The portion is small (double the portion shown in photo), but the taste of it worth the price!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Random find while I was walking to One Nimman. Ordered the Khao Soi Kai, really good! Noodles cooked just nicely in a thick curry broth.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
Authentic Chiang Mai style with classic jazzy playing at the background. Food is good with small portion.

Location just right opposite Nimman One. Worth to try if you want some peaceful meal although beside the main road, you can enjoy it without hassle.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Clinching star dishes for the day will be fried Sea bass fish and fried kway teow Rest are all good and authentic dishes . Lemon grass drink however was abit diluted for today .

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: I tried this restaurant on the recommendation of a Thai colleague, and I was very glad I did. It has a cozy atmosphere and nice food, and it was very easy to order food from the English-friendly menu using the numbering system. Staff weren't especially friendly, but they were efficient and food came quickly and was served hot. I'm not an expert on Thai food, but this met/ exceeded my expectations in taste terms and I'd be very happy to eat there again given the chance.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Nice reasonably priced Thai Food . They can adjust the spiciness.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Very nice place, perfect for tasting the North - Thai style food. Really broad menu with all the classics and local options, and many variants of sets for 2 or more people - all that with very reasonable prices. Atmosphere inside was also really nice, as mentioned

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:

Khao Soi with chicken take away was delicious.  Well worth another visit.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: This is a nice little cute restaurant,  a little run down, but service was friendly, food was delicious.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn

Review_Text: Best traditional thai food with affordable prices in town. Quantities are a bit small, so you might consider ordering two main dishes, and it's still cheaper than the rest of the restaurants around here.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: If you want to go back in time and enjoy solid Thai food...this has got to be it. Khao soi is delicious among many others in the extensive menu.❤️🇹🇭 …

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Excellent restaurant - the food is delicious and they have a great variety of options, all very reasonable priced too. Always very welcomed and the service is great!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Amazing restaurant! Great food at a cozy atmosphere. Probably the best price value you can get at Chiang Mai for an authentic Thai cuisine.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: อาหารอร่อย บรรยากาศ เหมือนทานบ้านญาติ อบอุ่น ไปเชียงใหม่ อย่าพลาดร้านห้องแถวนะครับ

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Food is great, though service can be a bit slow. Otherwise amazing vibes in this old school set up.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: The taste is not bed. But the service of servers are soooooo bed. We orderd 4 pad thai but had only one. They should have apologized to us when they were missed our order.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Food is good, but theres a bald staff kept looking at us like hes anxious for us to pay quickly? haha, he should relax, theres no customers now, literally empty except us.  I wouldnt come again

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text:
Okay but nothing to write home about.
Settings are nice and cozy but food is not as tasteful as it should be. Looks like a good place to bring the Thai in-laws though

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: It's a family style restaurant. Good Thai food and service. Highly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Service, food, ambiance all excellent. Prices very reasonable. Great place to get hard to find local dishes. Always make a point to visit when in town....

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Friendly, but the service was a bit slow. Food was ok. Fine for an everyday meal, but if you're looking for something special, there are other places to try.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: อร่อยที่สุดในนิมมาน อร่อยมากครับ

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Good Thai and Northern Thai food.. reasonably priced. Good friend that have lived here 20 years say it is one of their favorite places.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ไปกินร้านนี้แต่งร้านสไตล์เหมือนกินอาหารบ้านเพื่อนมีที่จอดรถอยู่ด้านหลังร้านแต่มีจำกัดรสชาติอาหารอร่อยราคาโอเค

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Slow service. Average food.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: อาหารรสชาติปกติ ราคาไม่เเพง

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Good flavour, but the ingredients weren't great.  My meat was mostly fat and skin and there wasnt much of that.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: We stopped here for lunch and my wife and I really enjoyed the food. She had the chicken pad Thai, I ate the chicken curry plate, we would recommend both.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Good value for Thai food in a convenient location.  It’s not the fanciest but it doesn’t need to be.  Fair prices and nice owners!

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Lovely northern Thai style restaurant in walking distance to the Maya shopping centre with exquisite cuisine and friendly service.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: อาหารโบราณหาทานยาก

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Amazing. Just the right portions, nothing was bad. Ordered things multiple times. Get the sausage. You won’t regret.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Try the fried egg and the salted fish

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Delicious food, friendly service, good price. In a nice cozy environment. Would definitely visit again.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: This is one of my favorite Thai restaurant in Chiang Mai, recommend deep fried ferment pork, pork grilled, and Pad Thai

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Traditional Thai food tastes super delicious , the place seems classic like the kind of restaurant from 20 years ago but not in a bad way.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ร้านนี้กินอาหารมาหลายสิบปี ไม่เคยผิดหวังเลย ชอบมาก อร่อย สะอาด สงบ ร้านก็สวยงาม

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: First restaurant we ate at when we arruved in CM 13+years ago.  Fiid is still great.  Unfortunately you must park along tge curb in front of the restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Old style Northern Thai food restaurant.  Well known and frequented by locals.  If you think you know that food.  Expect a different experience.  A place to experiment.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: ร้านข้าวแกงมีอาหารให้เลือกหลากหลาย ราคาเบา ๆ อร่อยมาก ชอบแกงแคสุด ๆ อยู่ถนนนิมมาน

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Excellent food, local specialties which you will not find in Bangkok or elsewhere. Nice interior as well, with some antique clocks and other stuff.

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: อาหารรสชาติดี อร่อย ไทยๆเหมือนแม่ทำกับข้าวให้ทาน ถูกใจมากค่ะ

Restaurant_Name: Hong Tauw Inn
Review_Text: Salad was sour (in a rotten way not a tasty way) and noodles were not flavorful. Then 12 hours later I experienced food poisoning. Avoid!

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: ห้องอาหารสะอาด โปร่งสบาย ไม่แออัด เหมาะกับการมาทานมื้อพิเศษ อาหารมีครบทั้งไทย ญี่ปุ่น สเต็ก และก็ของหวาน รสชาติมาตรฐานโรงแรมระดับสูง การจัดวางอาหารดูน่าทานและสะอาด พนักงานเติมของตลอดไม่มีขาด แนะนำให้จองโต๊ะมาก่อนถ้ามาช่วงวันหยุดครับ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
อาหารทะเลดี กุ้งสดจับเองแล้วให้ร้านย่างให้ หอยนางรมตัวใหญ่สดมาก อาหารทะเลอื่นๆมีให้เลือกเยอะ
อาหารปรุงสุก ให้ตักเอง หรือสั่ง อร่อย มีหลากหลาย
ชาชิมิ ต้องไปออเดอร์ไว้ให้พนักงานทำ ชิ้นเล็กไป และรอนาน
อาหาร ขนมอื่นๆ สามารถสั่งได้
ของหวานเยอะ น่ากิน มีไอติมแม็กนั่มรวมในบุฟ
มีดนตรีสดให้ฟัง
ห้องน้ำสะอาด ดีไซน์เก๋..
มีที่จอดรถ แต่เดินไกลหน่อย

เหมาะกับคนชอบอาหารทะเล นั่งชิลๆ จิบไวน์?
ถ้าจะเน้นบุฟเฟ่ต์ จะเดินเยอะจนเหนื่อย อิ่มก่อน

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: อาหารหลากหลาย ราคาไม่แพงนะถ้าเทียบกับคุณภาพของอาหารถือว่าดีเลยละ กุ้ง หอย ปู ปลา เนื้อ หมู ไก่ มีให้เลือเยอะพอประมาณโดยเฉพาะกุ้งตัวเป็นๆและตัวใหญ่  ก่อนจะไปควรโทรจองโต๊ะไว้ก่อนนะครับ เพราะคนค่อนข้างเยอะพอสมควร สถานที่จอดรถก็เยอะ เข้าได้ทั้ง 2 ทางต้องได้ไปอีกแน่นอนครับ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: ร้านอยู่ชั้น 1 ประเภท Buffet นั่งรับประทานได้ตั้งแต่เวลา 18.00-22.00 น. เท่ากับ4 ชั่วโมง กุ้ง หอย ปู ปลา อาหารต่าง ๆ และไอศครีมแมกนั่ม กินแบบจุใจไส้แตกไปเลย น้อง ๆ พนักงานน่ารัก บริการดี หยิบเก็บถ้วยจานตลอด เหมาะสมกับราคาที่จ่ายไปจริง ๆ พออิ่มแล้ว คาวจัง ยัดไม่ลง แทบอ๊วก ฮ่าฮ่าฮ่า

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: อาหารดีครับ อาหารทะเลสดๆ กุ้งจับตัวเป็นๆได้เลยครับ เป็นบุฟเฟ่ต์นานสชาติ มีครบทั้งญี่ปุ่น ฝรั่ง ไทย อาหารทะเล ของหวานก็มีอร่อยครับ ไอติม HagenDaz ด้วยครับ ที่จอดรถต้องจอดที่ตึกจอดรถของวันนิมมาน แล้วเดินมาครับ มี Stamp ที่จอดได้ฟรีครับ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
This is my third visit, and I met Bas again — his service is consistently excellent every time. He is very attentive, friendly, and truly makes guests feel welcome 🤍

I visited Eat at Rincome Chiang Mai and was impressed by the cozy and relaxing atmosphere. It’s perfect for coming with friends, family, or just chilling.

The food is delicious, made with fresh and high-quality ingredients 👍

Special compliments to Bas and the team for the great service. If you’re looking for a nice restaurant around Rincome, I highly recommend this place. I will definitely come back again 🤍

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: บุฟเฟ่ต์ คนละ 850 บาท net กินได้ตั้งแต่ 5 โมงเย็น จนถึง 4 ทุ่ม มาวันศุกร์ก่อนสงกรานต์ คนไม่เต็ม เน้นอาหารทะเล มีอาหารทะเลสดๆ กุ้ง ปู หอยหวาน หอยเชล หอยแมลงภู่ ที่เลือกแล้วเอาไปให้เชฟเผา ต้ม นึ่งได้, มีพวกทะเล on ice กุ้ง กั้ง หอยนางรมสด ไม่ค่อยได้กิน, น้ำจิ้มทะเลมี 3 แบบ ดั้งเดิม ผสมมัสตาร์ด ผสมวาซาบิ โดยส่วนตัวชอบอันหลัง, ซูชิ ปลาดิบมี, พิซซ่า กับอาหารปรุงสุก มีไม่เยอะ แต่ อร่อยมาก สั่งข้าวผัดมันเนื้อมาดีงาม น้องๆ พนักบริการดี ช่วยเทขยะทิ้งบ่อยๆ ช่วยคุณแม่ยกจานกลับมาที่โต๊ะด้วย เครื่องดื่มไม่ค่อยหลากหลาย มีแค่น้ำอัดลม กับน้ำหวาน ส่วนของหวานอลังการมาก ฟองดูช็อค เค้ก ขนมไทย ไอติมฮาเก้นดาส อิ่มจุกๆ ควรไปลองค่ะ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: อาหารสดใหม่ สะอาดคนชอบอาหารทะเลสดมีหอยนางรม ปู กุ้งแม่น้ำ กินแบบสะใจไม่อั้นเท่าที่กินไหวมีอาหารนานาชาติ ราคาแบบบุพเฟต์ถูกใจคนชอบกิน มีน้ำรีฟิวตลอด น้ำอัดลม น้ำเก็กฮวยถ้าชอบชาร้อนก็มีเติมได้ตลอด ฟังเพลงสดมีนักร้องมาร้องเพลงหวานๆๆ แอร์เย็น

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: บุฟเฟ่ต์ในราคาคนละ 850 บาท รวมทุกอย่างจบแล้วไม่มีบวกเพิ่ม บรรยากาศดีมากโดยเฉพาะช่วงปลายปีแบบนี้ เพราะเป็นช่วงเทศกาลก็จะได้ฟิวปาร์ตี้นิดหน่อย มีแต่มื้อเย็นนะคะตั้งแต่ 5 โมงถึง 4 ทุ่มเท่านั้น ในช่วงเทศกาลถ้าหากจองมาจะดีมาก โดยรวมถือว่าอาหารสะอาดถูกปาก กับราคานี้เหมาะสมแล้วค่ะ อาหารมีให้เลือกหลากหลายมาก เธอบ้ามานั่งทานกับครอบครัว หรือมีโอกาสพิเศษก็เหมาะสม เป็นทางเลือกให้กับทุกคนได้นะคะ ห้องแอร์ สะอาด มีเพลงให้ฟังอีกต่างหาก แนะนำว่ามาลองได้ค่ะ หวยส่วนตัวจอดรถข้างฮิลล์ไซด์คอนโด ฝั่งตรงข้ามค่ะเพราะว่าจอดฟรี เดินมานิดเดียวก็ถึง อีกอย่างก็คือเข้าออกง่าย แต่ถ้าใครสะดวกจอดรถจุดอื่นก็ตามสบายค่ะ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
ดีมาก​ คุ้มค่า​  บริการดี​ สะอาด
อาหาร​สดมาก​ กุ้งตัวโตๆๆๆ
มีดนตรีฟังแนว90

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
โดดเด่นอาหารทะเล กุ้งสดๆเป็นๆแน่นๆ หอย หมึก กั้ง ปู มีทั้งต้มแล้วและสด
ตักมาแล้วเลือกให้ปรุง ย่าง ผัด ต้ม ลวก
ซาชิมิ แซลมอน ทูน่า ซาบะ
เนื้อย่าง หมู่ย่าง ไส้กรอง
อาหารสุกอีกหลายอย่าง ขาหมูเยอรมัน ฯลฯของหวานมีเยอะ เฉาก๊วยเฉยๆ
บริการดี ดูแลดี
เครื่องดื่มมีน้อย 3 อย่าง น้ำเปล่า น้ำอัญชัน น้ำอัดลม

Outstanding seafood, fresh shrimp, clams, squid, lobster, crab, both boiled and fresh
Scoop it up and choose to cook, grill, stir fry, boil, boil
Sashimi, salmon, tuna, saba
Grilled beef, grilled pork,
Many other cooked dishes, German pork knuckle, etc.
Good service
There are few drinks, 3 types: plain water, butterfly pea water, soft drinks

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: บุฟเฟ่ต์​อาหารทะเล​ หัวละ​ 650 net คุ้มมากๆ​ อาหารอร่อย​ วัตถุดิบ​ดีงามสดใหม่​ ปลานึ่งซีอิ้วอร่อยมากๆ​ แซลม่อนดองน้ำปลาก็ดี​ ปลาผัดขึ้นฉ่ายอร่อย​​ เมนูส่วนใหญ่สั่งผ่านแอพของทางร้าน​ อาหารมาเร็วมาก​ นอกจาก​ a la carte แล้วยังมีไลน์อาหารให้ไปตักเอง​ มีสลัด​ ซุปไทย​ ซุปฝรั่ง​ ข้าว​ แล้วก็ขนมที่มีทั้งขนมไทยแล้วก็เค้ก​ ตอนเราไปขนมไทยมีตะโก้เผือกที่อร่อยมาก​ ส่วนเค้ก​ red velvet ก็อร่อย​ แต่ที่ชอบสุดคือเค้กมูสมะม่วงที่ไปตักมา​ 2 รอบแบบจุกๆ​ นอกจากอาหารดี​ บรรยากาศดี​ ราคาดีแล้ว​ พนักงานที่นี่ก็ดีสุดๆ​ บริการดีดุจญาติมิตร​ คะแนนเต็ม​ 100  ก็ให้ได้ทั้ง​ 100 ได้เลย​ ไว้ไปอุดหนุนอีก

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
อาหารถือว่าคุ้มค่าในราคานี้ครับ ติดอย่างเดียวถ้วยน้ำจิ้มเล็กไปหน่อย
พนักงานบริการดีมากก
บรรยากาศดี ดีอีกอย่างคือคิดราคาจากอายุเด็ก
ราคาเด็ก 4-11 ขวบ 400
ต่ำกว่านั้นฟรี
ผมมากินช่วง Oktoberfest ถ่ายมายังไม่หมดนะครับเหลือเมนูอีกประมาณหนึ่ง

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: อาหารอร่อยคุ้มราคา ซีฟู๊ด สดและใหม่ มา 2 รอบ แล้วคะ ร้องเพลงได้เพราะมากๆคะ บรรยากาศดี  และพนักงานน่ารักทุกคน บริการดีเยี่ยมคะ โดยเฉพาะ ไข่เจียว กับ ชิน คะ กลับมาใหม่แน่นอนคะ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: อาหารอร่อยมาก พนักงานบริการดีมากๆค่ะ สุภาพไพเราะมาก พนักงงานวันนี้สี บาส ไข่เจียว ชิน ปลาย

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: การบริการดีมากค่ะ พนักงานใส่ใจยิ้มแย้มแจ่มใส ส่วนอาหารรสชาติไม่จัดจ้านมาก แต่คุณภาพดีค่ะ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: ไฮไลต์คือกุ้งแม่น้ำเป็น ๆ ให้ตักแบบไม่อั้น เลือกให้ทำเมนูอะไรก็ได้ แต่ขอแนะนำเป็นกุ้งเผาเพราะอร่อยมากๆ เนื้อแน่น ฟินมากครับ หากินในเชียงใหม่ยากด้วย เมนูอื่นก็รสชาติดีโดยเฉพาะซาชิมิ เนื้อย่าง และไก่ซอสครีม (ติดนิดเดียวตรงที่อาหารทะเลที่ทำสุกแล้วตรงกลางห้อง เย็นไปหน่อยครับ ถ้าทำให้อุ่นกว่านี้ได้จะอร่อยขึ้นอีกมากๆ) การบริการดีเยี่ยม พนักงานยิ้มแย้ม คอยเก็บจาน/เคลียร์โต๊ะให้เสมอ พนักงานที่สเตชั่นต่างๆ ก็อัธยาศัยดี และปรุงอาหารได้อร่อยมากครับ ขอขอบคุณและให้กำลังใจพี่บาส พี่ไข่เจียว พี่ชิน พี่นุ่น พี่อาร์ม และพี่แท็กซี่ สำหรับการบริการอย่างดีเยี่ยมนะครับ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
Fresh food with various types of menus.
The service is extremely good.
The atmosphere is great with acoustic music around 18:00.

But I recommend coming early to ensure a peaceful meal. :)

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Came here for breakfast buffet 😋 alot to choose from👍👍💯, has more outside noodle stalls ect, their international seafood buffet also looks good but unfortunately I didn't have time to try it. …

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Me and my partner have our anniversary dinner at this restaurant and it is the most wonderful experience we ever have, the food is fantastic but the service is next level, waiters and waitress are very friendly and excellent at what they did. It's officially in our must go list for the next time we come to Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: คุณปาย ไข่เจียว อาร์ม แท็ก และคุณนุ่นบริการดีมากครับ อาหารก็อร่อย สด ประทับใจแล้วจะกลับมาอีกครับ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: บุฟเฟต์มื้อเย็น 290.- รวมน้ำเปล่าและเครื่องดื่มสมุนไพร(มีเก็กฮวย) อาหารสลับเปลี่ยนหมุนเวียน หลักๆเป็นอาหารไทย อิตาเลี่ยน มีปลาผัดผงกะหรี่ ซุปต้มยำปลา ซุปฟักทอง ปลาย่างมะนาว ยำ ลาบ ทาโกะยากิ สุกียากี้(สั่งทำร้อนๆ) สปาเก็ตตี้/มักโรนี(สั่งทำใหม่ๆร้อนๆ)มีให้เลือกว่าจะทานแบบ ซอส หรือ คาโบนาร่า มีลาซานญ่าผักโขมชีส ฯลฯ รสชาติโอเค อร่อยและอิ่ม คุ้มกับราคาที่จ่าย มีขนม ของหวาน หวานเย็น เค้ก พานาคอตต้า (เค้กส้มอร่อยมากกก) มีสลัดบาร์ให้เลือกตักเอง พนักงานคอยเติมน้ำ เก็บจานใช้แล้วเรื่อยๆ พนักงานบริการดีครับ โดยรวม ถือว่า คุ้มค่ากับราคาที่จ่ายไป จอดรถอาคาร one nimman แล้วเดินมาที่ห้องอาหาร สะดวกครับ จอดฟรี3ชั่วโมง และ สามารถนำบัตรจอดรถมาสแตมป์ตราได้ครับ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: น่าจะมีให้กด 0 ดาวนะ ไปสองคนกินบุฟเฟ่ต์บาบีคิวซีฟู๊ด หยิบกุ้งให้ย่างตอนหกโมงสิบ เอามาเสริฟตอนทุ่มครึ่ง สรุปได้กินกุ้งเผาเฉลี่ยคนละสามตัว เพราะถ้าไปตักอีกรอบคงมาเสริฟสามทุ่ม  มาตราฐานทำได้แค่นี้ก็อุตส่ามาเปิดบุฟเฟ่ต์บาบีคิวซีฟู๊ด  เรียกำนักงานมาแจ้งปัญหาคำตอบคือมีคนร้องเรียนแบบนี้บ่อยๆ แต่ไม่รู้แก้ยังไง

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
ชอบขนมหวานที่นี่มาก จะเป็นจานแรกทุกครั้งที่มากิน 😋 รสชาดอาหารอร่อยพอเหมาะพอดีแบบคนไทยหรือชาวต่างชาติกินอร่อย บรรยากาศสบายๆ พนักงานน่ารักทุกท่านเลยชอบมาก 😊

ตัด 🌟 เพราะว่าอาหารประเภทเนื้อย่างมีบางวันเท่านั้น คือที่เปลี่ยนมีไก่ย่างและเนื้อย่าง ไปมากกว่า 4-5 ครั้งเจอสลับกัน 2 อย่างนี้เอง(ความคิดเห็นส่วนตัวนะคะ)

สรุปรวมๆ คือราคานี้ 290฿(รวมน้ำดื่ม) เลิศค่ะแนะนำไปลองเลย

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: ไปกิน พฤ , อาทิ คนน้อย 850.- ไม่มีบวก อาหาร ดีทุกอย่าง รวมทั้งของหวานด้วย คุ้มสุดๆๆ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Advanced reservations are recommended.   The best Seafood buffet in Chiangmai.  799- net.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: อาหารทะเลสดมากๆ เลยค่ะ มีให้เลือกหลากหลายด้วย คุ้มมากๆ สะอาด บรรยากาศดี และน้องๆ พนักงานก็บริการดีมากๆเลยค่ะ สุภาพ ยิ้มแย้มตลอด ขอชื่นชมน้องๆ กลุ่มนี้ที่มาบริการเป็นพิเศษนะคะ น้องปลาย ไข่เจียว ชิน นุ่น และ ทิศค่ะ

Review_Text: มาบุฟเฟต์ ตั้งใจจะมาทานอาหารทะเล อาหารทะเลไม่สดเลย กินไปเอ๊ะไป ปลาหมึกกลิ่นแปลกๆ ปูกลิ่นแรงขึ้นจมูกเบย แซลมอนซูชิอร่อย ปกติ ส่วนด้านพนักงานบริการดีมาก เป็นกันเอง น่ารัก ขอติแค่เรื่องอาหารจริงๆ เพราะตั้งใจมาทานอย่างมีความสุข แต่กินเสร็จกลับบ้านแบบงงๆ 5555

Review_Text: ไม่ได้เข้าไปทานในห้องอาหาร แต่เห็นพนักงานยืนขายอยู่ริมถนนประมาณว่าซื้อกลับบ้าน  ผ่านมาสามสี่วันก็ยังเห็นอยู่  อยากรู้ก็เลยแวะดู  ได้ของกินตามคาด  รสชาติพอทานได้  ราคาไม่แพง  พนักงานบริการดีมาก  หรือจะเอาขึ้นไปนั่งทานข้างบนก็ได้

Review_Text: ไปลองทานบุฟเฟต์อาหารเย็นราคา 290 บาท ซึ่งเป็นราคาไม่แพง และอาหารก็มีให้เลือกเหมาะสมกับราคา มีมุมของหวาน, เค้ก, ข้าวซอย, มุมกับข้าว 4-5 อย่าง, ของทานเล่น รู้สึกว่าสมราคา. การบริการดี

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
SEAFOOD BUFFET IS MY BIG HAPPINESS.  HERE'S MY P A R A D I S E :)

Once I stepped in, my inner voice shouted "W  o w ww w !" And I hurried beeline to the steamed crabs.  LOL.

Review_Text: ซีฟ๊ด บุฟเฟ่ต์ เชียงใหม่ ต้องยกให้ที่นี่เลย มีกุ้งเป็นๆ ด้วย น้องพนักงานบริการดีมาก น่ารักมาก

Review_Text: อาหารหลากหลาย อาหารทะเลสดใหม่ มีคุณภาพ คุ้มกับราคา

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: มาพักที่โรงแรมนี้เป็นประจำ วันนี้รีบเลยใช้บริการอาหารกลางวันที่ร้านนี้ก่อนออกไปข้างนอก คุณแม่สั่งผัดกระเพรา ข้าวไม่สุก กินไม่หมด เหลือเกือบเต็มจาน ตัวเองสั่งชุดขันโตก รสชาติก็โอเค จัดจานมาดูอลังการ แต่พอเคี้ยวไส้อั่วไปทีเดียว กรวดทรายมาเต็มๆ คำ ขณะที่นั่งทานก็แมลงวันบินว่อน ไม่ชอบเขียนรีวิวเชิงลบกับที่ไหนๆ แค่ผิดหวังในฐานะลูกค้าประจำของโรงแรมนี้ ราคาขนาดนี้ มาตรฐานอาหารของคุณตัน ควรทำได้ดีกว่านี้ค่ะ

Review_Text: อาหารมีให้เลือกเยอะ หลากหลาย ของทะเลสด พนักงาน น้อง..ไข่เจียว บาส ชิน ปลาย บริการดี สรุปคือประทับใจ จะมาใช้บริการอีกนะคะ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Pleasant hotel casual dining room, buffet dinners on some nights, service is efficient, food is ok but the clientele needs improving. Right now it's a good value as the hotel is new, but see what happens in a few months.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Best value dinner buffet! I and my wife tried dinner buffet with little expectation but alas, turned out to be a pleasant surprise. Would absolutely recommend this place not just because of the sheer dollar value but also because of the variety of foods, quality service, and great hospitality from the restaurant crew.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
บุฟเฟต์อาหารทะเล ญี่ปุ่น ไม่จำกัดเวลา
อาหารทะเลสดมาก พนักงานปิ้งย่างให้ เลือกที่นั่งได้ทั้งห้องแอร์ และ ด้านนอก
จอดรถได้ที่ตึกจอดรถแล้วเอาบัตรมาแสตมป์ที่ร้าน ทานเสร็จสามารถเดินเที่ยวต่อได้ ของหวานหลากหลาย อร่อยดี มาที่เดียวคุ้ม

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: ตามมาจากTikTok เห็นหลายๆช่องโปรโมทเยอะแล้วดูน่ากินเลยตัดสินใจไปกิน แต่พอไปถึงร้านเท่านั้นแหละ omg อาหารทุกอย่างที่อยู่ตรงหน้ามันไม่สมเหตุสมผลกับราคา 850 บาทเลย อาหารตามตลาดนัดยังดูดีแล้วก็สดอร่อยกว่านี้อีก ทุกอย่างจืดชืด เย็นกินไม่อร่อยเลย ปกติไปกินบุฟเฟ่ต์หลายที่มากไม่เคยเจอที่ไหนที่ไปกินแล้วรู้สึกแย่มาก่อน อยากจะบอกว่าอย่าเชื่อใน TikTok เยอะ ของจริงต่างจากที่โฆษณามาก

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Great food! Mixture of Thai and western buffett. For the price it can't be beaten! Plus all the wonderful desserts alone are worth the price.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: กุ้งเผาดีมากแต่รอเผานานไปหน่อยพวกขนมเค้กกับไอติมดีสุดๆแต่ที่แย่คือแซลมอนซาชิมิ ไม่ค่อยสดหั่นไม่สวยติดหนังเลี่ยนมาก ส่วนอาหารปรุงสุกรสชาติเฉยๆ

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
The food was excellent, especially the shrimp—it was very delicious.
I would also like to commend the staff: Bas, Pai, Khai Jiew, and Mix. They were all very professional, courteous, and provided outstanding service.
Thank you for a wonderful dining experience.
👍🏻

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
ที่เลือกมาเพราะเห็นว่าเป็นยู แต่รสชาติกินไม่ได้เลย สปาเกตตี้พริกแห้งมันคือเส้นที่ลวกน้ำร้อนแล้วแค่โรยพริกไทยเฉยๆ ไม่มีรสชาติใดๆ สเต๊กไม่มีรสเกลือ รสพริกไทยเลย อาหารก็ช้า ถ้าจะขายในราคาถูกคุณต้องคำนึงสิ่งที่ลูกค้าจะได้จาก U nimman ด้วย ไม่ใช่นึกอยากคิดโปร ตั้งราคาถูกๆ เรียกลูกค้า พนงเต็มห้องอาหารแต่ไม่บริการ น้ำลูกค้าหมดแก้วแล้ว ไม่มีใครมาเติม ไม่มีใครมาถาม เห็นมีสเตชั่นน้ำแต่โรงแรมระดับนี้ไม่นี้ไม่น่าเหมาะกับ self service
ผิดหวังมาก จะไม่กลับมาอีก

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
Firstly, the buffet is much smaller than it looks in pictures. Actually is smaller than all seafood buffets I have been to here.
Seafood quality  is good but cold, actually very cold because they put it on the ice. I personally don't like eating e.g. prawns  like they were just taken from a fridge.
They have also some desserts but they are far from quality desserts, rather cheap ones similar to ones, you see at night markets.
In general, I think the buffet is too expensive and as a result far from being a good deal. But seafood quality is good.

Update:
Salmon, tuna used to be good, now is absolutely tasteless.
If someone covered my eyes I couldn't even guess it's a salmon or tuna.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: เป็นร้านบุฟเฟต์อาหารทะเล เชียงใหม่ ที่มีอาหารหลากหลายที่สุด โซนของหวานอร่อยมาก จัดอาหารสวยงามน่ากิน สถานที่ดีมากๆ อาหารทะเลความสดกลางๆ มีกุ้งเป็นๆถือว่าดี คุ้มค่ามาก

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Last Thursday, I had a marvellous seafood dinner buffet at Eat@Rincome inside the U Nimman Chiang Mai Hotel. Khun Kai-Jiew's outstanding table service greatly enhanced the evening. Her prompt and professional responses to my requests were a pleasure to watch. There was a great variety of seafood and pasta dishes. Highly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: คุ้มค่าคุ้มราคามากค่ะ กับ บุฟเฟต์อาหารทะเล ที่เชียงใหม่ ราคาเบาๆคุ้มมาก โซน อาหารทะเล อาหารไทย ขนมหวาน ไทย-เทศ คุ้มสุดคุ้ม ให้ผ่านมาต้องแวะะมาค่ะ  ซีฟู๊ดบุฟเฟต์นิมมาน บุฟเฟ่ต์ซีฟู้ด เชียงใหม่.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: ห้องอาหารสวย สะอาด พนักงานบริการดีมาก อาหารอร่อย

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
The Food variety is good. The salad is great you can also mixed salad within a blow, but the service is not so good. I got spoiled food and I called the waiter. They do not even apologize. 😳 The bread is great and would be awesome if there was a toaster 😶
The deep fried plates were quite hard especially for the nuggets. I think they sit there quite a while.
The Thai desserts is fine . It is a nice place , but     It need to improve many things especially the service.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text:
The restaurant is great, with a lot of Thai and Western dishes from starters to desserts.
The service is absolutely fantastic.

I've been there many times and was never disappointed.

Set Lunch for 190 Baht everyday.
Dinner for 300 Baht during the week and 400 during the weekend.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Good place and location, good and kind service, but food is not that good. It is average. Nothing special and the shrimps i think were old and bad taste. The Khao Soi was half good compared to a street vendor one. Very good price. Good place if you do not have alternatives. Desserts and fruit are good. Bread is very goos and they do have a toaster. I think this place has a lot of potential, they really have to do a better job on the food, maybe also rising the price. I would pay double if food is above average.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: เสียดายเงินค่ะ รูปไม่ตรงปก โทถามบอกมีสเต๊กเนื้อในรูปโฆษณาก็มี กุ้งตัวนิดเดียวเท่าหมูกะทะที่ไปกินอาหารฝรั่งมีอย่งเดียวผักโขมอบชีสหัวหละ690แต่คุณภาพไม่น่าเกิน400แถมเช็คบิลบวกเซอรวิสชารท์อีกเรากินบิลออกมา5000 แต่จ่ายรวมบวกไปอีก898ฟรีฟรีที่เสียรวมเกือบหกพันพาลูกสาวไปเลี้ยง12ขวบก็คิดราคาผู้ใหญ่..นอนไม่หลับเลยเสียดายรู้งี้กินชาบู199คุ้มกว่าเยอะ

Review_Text: อาหารทะเลดี บรรยากาศเยี่ยม พนักงานบริการดี

Review_Text: โฆษนาว่าเป็นอาหารบุฟเฟ่ต์นานาชาติฟังดูแล้วเลิศอลังการน้ะค้ะแต่ไม่มีสเต๊กเนื้อ ไม่มีพิซซ่า ไม่มีสปาเก็ตตี้ อาหารญี่ปุ่นมีแต่ซูชิปลาสด อูด้งหรืออื่นๆไม่มี อาหารไม่มีช้อยไห้เลือกมากนัก ไม่ประทับใจ ไม่อยากกลับไปอีก รูปภาพกับราคาสูงและดูมีระดับกว่าความเป็นจริงมากๆ ไม่สมควรจะเรียกว่าบุฟเฟ่ต์นานาชาติด้วยซำ้

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Their weekend seafood buffet is pretty good with quite a few options including some made-to-order stations. It's not the best quality you'll find but it's decent enough and if you want a good buffet without the high price tag of Shangri La or Le Meridien this might be a good option.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: Not perfect but satisfied.  For 290 baht it's all good. But I still feel the lunch buffet at Chiang mai grand view hotel is more better. Anyway we enjoyed our dinner. Cheers.

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: อาหารสดมาก หลากหลาย พนักงานบริการดี

Restaurant_Name: Eat@Rincome
Review_Text: For 50 baht more than you would spend for a set a McDonalds, you get a nice all-you-can eat buffet. is the food 5-star fare? Of course not. Is food quality equal to or better than most restaurants in the area, absolutely.

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text:
🎶 Sax Music House ร้านบาร์-ดนตรีสดในโครงการ One Nimman ที่สายชิลต้องแวะ!
บรรยากาศดี ดนตรีเพราะ อาหารและเครื่องดื่มครบ เหมาะกับมานั่งฟังเพลงกับเพื่อนมาก ๆ

✨ สิ่งที่ชอบ
• ดนตรีสดดีมาก แนวแจ๊ส/ป๊อป/โซล ฟังเพลินทั้งคืน
• อาหารหลากหลาย สั่งแบบเป็นเซ็ตก็คุ้ม
• ค็อกเทลและไวน์รสชาติดี
• ทำเลดี อยู่ในนิมมาน เดินง่าย บรรยากาศดีตอนค่ำ

⚠️ ข้อควรรู้
• คนเยอะช่วงศุกร์-เสาร์ ควรจองหรือไปเร็ว
• เสียงดังนิดหน่อย เพราะเป็นบาร์ดนตรีสด

🎶 Sax Music House, located inside One Nimman, is a must-visit bar for anyone who loves live music.
Great atmosphere, talented musicians, tasty food, and a nice cocktail selection.

✨ What I loved
• High-quality live bands (jazz, pop, soul)
• Wide food selection + value-for-money combo sets
• Good cocktails and wine
• Perfect location in the heart of Nimman

⚠️ Things to know
• Gets crowded on weekends — better to book or come early
• Noise level can be loud (live music bar)

มีวงเล่นสดเป็นช่วงๆ เสียงดนตรีดีมาก ไม่ดังเกินไป นั่งคุยกับเพื่อนก็ยังสบาย เมนูเครื่องดื่มมีทั้งค็อกเทล เบียร์ และม็อกเทล ใครอยากได้ฟีลบาร์เท่ๆ ไม่วุ่นวายเกินไป แนะนำเลย

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text:
Absolutely incredible experience from start to finish. The atmosphere is stunning—warm, inviting, and the perfect place to relax and enjoy the night. The food is beyond good; every dish was thoughtfully prepared and packed with flavor.

Service was a 10/10—attentive, friendly, and never overbearing. A huge shoutout to our server Oley, who made the entire experience even better with genuine hospitality and great recommendations.

And the live music… hands down some of the best we’ve heard in Chiang Mai. Talented, soulful, and the perfect volume to elevate the vibe without overpowering conversation.

This place is a must-visit if you’re in Chiang Mai. We’ll absolutely be back.

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text: A strong shout out to the incredible service at this bar/restaurant where the servers outperform many servers at top restaurants in New York, London, Washington, and many other major cities we have visited. They were very attentive and polite without being overbearing. If you want better seating that is not yet available, they keep an eye out and move you as soon as they can. The managers here either hire great people, train them well, or both. We tipped accordingly. The food is good to very good. The one "but": The music is too loud, and many local Thai friends said they avoid the place for the same reason. If the decibels were lower and the music leaned a bit more to the ballad and melodic side, I would be there every night. Some of the bands have a lighter touch, especially earlier on in the evening. Well worth a visit though, despite this flaw.

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text:
**The Sax Restaurant at Ruamchok: Cozy Vibes, Great Music, and Amazing Cocktails**

If you're looking for a cozy spot to unwind with good food, great drinks, and live acoustic music, The Sax Restaurant at Ruamchok is the place to be. This charming venue offers a perfect combination of a relaxed atmosphere, excellent service, and a tempting promotion for cocktail lovers.

**Cozy and Clean with Friendly Service**

The moment you step into The Sax, you'll notice the warm, inviting ambiance that makes it a great spot for a casual meal or an evening out with friends. The interior is stylish yet comfortable, and the restaurant is always kept spotless. The staff is attentive and friendly, ensuring that every guest feels welcome and well taken care of.

**Happy Hour Promotion: Buy 1, Get 1 Free on Cocktails**

If you love cocktails, you’ll appreciate their special promotion—buy 1 cocktail and get 1 free before 8 p.m. It’s the perfect excuse to indulge in some of their expertly crafted drinks, whether you’re a fan of classic mojitos or prefer something more adventurous. With this great deal, you can enjoy quality cocktails without breaking the bank.

**Live Acoustic Music: The Best in Town**

What sets The Sax apart from other restaurants in the area is its live acoustic music. Every evening, talented local musicians perform soft acoustic sets, creating a relaxing and enjoyable atmosphere. Whether you’re there for dinner or just drinks, the music adds an extra layer of enjoyment to your experience.

**Conclusion**

The Sax Restaurant at Ruamchok is a hidden gem for anyone who enjoys good food, amazing cocktails, and live music. With its cozy atmosphere, clean space, excellent service, and a fantastic cocktail promotion, it’s a great place to unwind and enjoy the best acoustic tunes in town. Make sure to drop by before 8 p.m. to take advantage of their buy 1, get 1 free cocktail offer!

Review_Text:

Not sure if it’s because today is the weekly Friday White Market, but there were several bands performing tonight, and the atmosphere was fantastic.

Praise the waitress Pat; she was very polite, attentive to the needs of the guests, and extremely thoughtful. She even noticed an error on the bill which was very professional.

Highly recommended.

Review_Text: The atmosphere is cozy and intimate, making it the perfect spot to relax and enjoy some live jazz music. The drinks are reasonably priced and the staff are friendly and attentive. Overall, I had a fantastic experience at The Sax Music House and I highly recommend it to anyone visiting Chiang Mai.

Review_Text:

ราคาอาหารค่อยข้างสูงนิดนึง แต่ก็โปรโมชั่นที่ทางร้านแนะนำ

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text:
They made a very good cocktail and the price in the Happy Hour (2 cocktails in a price of one) was great. The food were ok. We ordered Roti with chicken green curry and they served the chicken in one big piece and the chicken was deep fried. It was really strange and difficult to eat. The pomelo salad was good. Maybe it’s more a place to sip a drink with some snacks than to have a dinner. The restroom are somewhere else which was every time like journey, especially for a woman.
The bands were good but much too loud. I’d expect the jazz, blues music bar to be cosy-chilly.

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text: My friend recommended this place for me to hang out together in the end we were having the best time of our life here. The music here are super awesome the best of the best probably going to be the drink i really love the choices of drink here and give it up for the jazz music everyone seem to enjoyed the feeling with thrill. Definitely and totally recommend!!

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text: Had an amazing time. Staff member Pen was amazing, very friendly and a kind warm smile. Brilliant music and a lovely buzz. It's on the side street to the centre which means it less noisy. Lovely decor and drinks are good.

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text:
Great vibe, great food, and great music, nothing better than these three combined, not to mention their top tier service.

For the food you have to get their SAX crispy pork, the crunch is out of this world.

PS: Band was also amazing!

10/10 Would come back

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text: I liked the cocktails and the bands were pretty good. Nice atmosphere and good service. I’d go back!

Review_Text:

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text: Sax music house is a great place to go for a drink and to listen to some music, but we went for dinner, and it was meh.  The menu is extensive with a huge mixture of Western and Thai choices.  This is usually a bad sign, and here the food was ok but nothing special as is usual with a place with this sort of menu...everything average, nothing outstanding.  Service was a bit off, with beer bottles arriving but no glasses... food orders duplicated, etc.  To be fair, we had a large group (8 pax), and the staff tried their best, but they seemed overwhelmed.  The music is very loud, so if you want to be able to have a conversation with the person across the table get a seat in the outdoor area.  Overall, it seems to cater to the tourist crowd... it's not bad, but locals can find better (and much cheaper drinks) nearby.

Restaurant_Name: The Sax Music House One Nimman
Review_Text:
Probably one of best service experiences in my life.  We had dinner here a few days ago.  You just twitch a finger and the staff come right to your table.  Amazing.  We had a reservation and the table was perfect. The food was outstanding in every respect. The cocktails are second to none.  We enjoyed a wonderful bottle of Carmener wine.
The music was great. Listened to 3 different bands over a 4 hour period. The vibes were awesome.  Love this place. We're going back there tonight.

Add in a big selection of food to pair with it all, and you’ve got a spot that’s hard to beat for a night out.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text: The Happy Frog is a true gem, especially if you're looking for something different in Chiang Mai. They have an incredible selection of imported cheeses, cold cuts, and a wonderful wine list. The food is top-notch, with the sandwiches and paninis being a must-try. The atmosphere is relaxed and perfect for a long lunch or a relaxed dinner.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text:
The atmosphere of The Happy Frog was quite nice, looks like a chill place to eat and the menu looked quite good too with decent prices. However, my wife and I came in about 10pm on a Monday and we sat ourselves with no one really helping us. After getting attention of a staff, she helped us but didn't seem that welcoming. She informed us no more food being served, which was OK and we ordered 2 drinks. After waiting for awhile, about 5 staff kept chatting near the cashier zone, which is fine, but it was loud and disturbing. So, we were going to move ourselves and we sat further inside. Then, a new staff comes up to us and asks what did we order after we waited for about 10 minutes. Instead, we just left because it was just a bad experience since the beginning. We don't blame the staff, the restaurant needs more awareness and training. Hopefully the future goes well as Nimman area always has up and coming new places that may overtake underperformers.

**Owner replied and seems positive in improving their brand! Very proactive from the owner and hope the place improves!

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text: First time being here. The staffs are really nice, always smile and help. Winelist is great. Not that expensive with nice test. I love the burrata dish and cream cheese in cold cut dish. They are so amazing. One of my top 3 restaurant in Chiangmai for my 6 days trip.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text:
I really enjoyed the cold cuts here — very tasty. I ordered the mixed frog platter and it was much bigger than I expected. I was full after that and couldn’t order anything else. The wine was also great.

The downside: the staff didn’t seem very professional, and the mussels weren’t very fresh.

Still, it’s a nice place with a good vibe. I’d definitely come back.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text:
Nice place to hang out and sip some inexpensive wine. The wine selection is quite ok and prices are definitely cheaper than in Bangkok. You can sit outside which is nice on a weekend because there is a market going on.

I was a bit overwhelmed by the food options. It‘s hard to say what their specialty is. They have Italian, Spanish, French and German produces. What we had was nice though. Just a plate with cold cuts and wine. Definitely a nice place to visit.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text: Stopped in to have dinner last night, what a wonderful meal. Great service, and very pleasant staff.   I had the grilled vegetables, with fresh salad. My wife had a grilled cheese sandwich, my daughter had a peperoni and grilled cheese sandwich.  Also had the garlic shrimp, and parmesan potato wedges. All the dishes were very tasty, and cooked to perfection. The atmosphere is very pleasant and inviting!!! Highly recommend this restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text: This is nice spot to sit and have some small eats. We had a sandwich and meat & cheese plate. The atmosphere was relaxing and the food was good. The staff were very friendly and helpful. I would definitely recommend coming here if you are looking for some comfort food or a glass of wine.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text:
This is a very nice place where you can eat good oysters at an affordable price and drink fine wine.

The service is impeccable and the spot is well situated in the heart of the Nimann One patio.

The have a very big selection of wines if you want to get your own bottles, otherwise the selection for wine by the glass is still working.

I am not sure if they have champagne.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text: บรรยากาศดี ชีส กับ cold cut ดีงาม มีไวน์ให้เลือกหลายตัว มีให้ชิมก่อนตัดสินใจ ราคาไม่แพงเวอร์ มีสองสาขา แต่ชอบสาขานี้มากกว่า

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text:
A must-visit place for anyone who loves wine.

-Reasonable wine prices compared to retail prices.
-List of wines in various price ranges, grape variety, and imported from various countries.
-Nice & delicious menu (ex. cheese platter).
-Outdoor and indoor tables where you can enjoy live music right next to you.
-Great service from the staff.

I will definitely visit again whenever I go to Chiang Mai again. If you like wine lover, highly recommend a visit na krup~!👍

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text: Very pretty place in the middle of One Nimman square. We had the beef blue cheese salad, it was amazing!! Fair prices, very attentive staff and great vibes overall. 100% recommend!

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text:
This is a nice spot at One Nimman and one of the few places in Chiang Mai to get good fresh oysters with a nice glass of wine. I just really wish they would teach their staff how to shuck oysters properly. I have had oysters here many times and they never cut the oysters loose from the shell, as you should when shucking an oyster. So instead the customer has to sit and try to loosen each oyster with a tiny spoon, which is quite annoying. Also, sometimes the oyster is completely mangled and cut to pieces because they have been moving the knife around inside too much.

They also have decent sandwiches although they are a bit stingy with the amount of meat and cheese they put in the sandwiches sometimes. Considering the price it would be nice if there was a bit more filling in the sandwiches.

If this review was helpful, please give it a 👍

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text: Located in One Nimman by the open courtyard. It has some inside seating, but most of the seating is outside. Their specialty seems to be sandwiches and they offer a variety of different types on their menu. I got one of their loaded fries. They have charcuterie boards, which would be good to share with friends. They didn't have the 2 items I initially wanted which sucked and then I found out they also don't accept credit cards, which limited me in what I could get. The prices are on the more expensive side for Chiang Mai, so I was surprised they don't accept credit cards. In my experience, the more expensive places typically take credit cards. The food was good but come prepared with a lot of cash if you don't want to be limited with what you can order.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text:
Style over substance. It has a nice atmosphere overlooking the square but the rest is disappointing.

The service is terrible, the waiting staff just disappear and ignore you.

The cheese and charcuterie platter looked good visually and was very generously portioned but was absolutely tasteless.  I'm not quite sure how they managed to find such dull tasting cheeses and how they managed to make ham taste of nothing. The only thing with any flavour was the chive dipping sauce that accompanied the bread.

The wines were equally disappointing. I had a glass of red Bordeaux which was vaguely recognisable as such but very tannic and poor quality and a glass of pinot noir that would be better used as paint thinner.

Avoid if you have any taste buds left.

Restaurant_Name: The happy frog
Review_Text:
Save yourself the misery and avoid this place. We ordered tomato soup, an egg dish and a bolognese cheese bread. The eggs came out first. So my companion ate first. When it was almost done, then my bread came. Soup came last.

My bolognese and the soup are sickeningly sweet. I don’t think it’s a tomato “sauce”, but more likely ketchup: For both— including the soup. Inedible. The service is very slow despite not being too busy. They have buttons at the table but I don’t think they work.

Expensive for what you get. Especially if you cant eat most of what comes out. It’s a nice place to sit so maybe just a drink to relax.

And no toilet!

Restaurant_Name: Skugga Estate - One Nimman Branch
Review_Text:
You won’t believe there’s a craft‑chocolate heaven hiding right here in Chiang Mai!

They grow their own cacao in Chiang Mai and turn it into mind-blowing chocolate.

The staff friendly, polite, and eager to walk you through every item. They let you sample every single chocolate bar while telling the story behind each one, and that’s exactly how I ended up gladly parting with my money for such top‑notch treats.

This stop was hands‑down the highlight of my Chiang Mai trip. Do yourself a favor: put this place on your must‑visit list. You’ll thank me later!

Restaurant_Name: Skugga Estate - One Nimman Branch

I ordered a hot green tea for THB75. Sat outside which was nice

Restaurant_Name: Skugga Estate - One Nimman Branch
Review_Text:
I recently had the pleasure of visiting a place that left a lasting impression. From the moment I walked in, I was captivated by the elegant and thoughtful design and decor. The attention to detail in creating a welcoming atmosphere was truly remarkable.

The culinary delights only added to the experience. Their chocolate was of the highest quality, a delectable treat that satisfied my sweet tooth. The freshly baked bread was a delight, and the food was simply outstanding. Every dish was a culinary masterpiece, and it was evident that a lot of care and skill went into their preparation.

What truly impressed me was the thoughtfulness that extended even to the silverware. It’s the little things that make a big difference, and in this place, every detail was carefully considered.

In summary, they did an exceptional job in creating an unforgettable dining experience. I can’t recommend it enough for those who appreciate not only great food but also the overall ambiance and attention to detail. This place is a true gem.

I had two sips and decided to leave it as this made me feel uncomfortable and rightly so as then shortly after I began having stomach pain. We came here for a drink before dinner and ended up being in pain and back and forth from the toilet all meal ruining the whole night. The next day I am still in pain.

Avoid at all costs, terrible people, service and establishment. DISGRACEFUL.

Restaurant_Name: ภัตตาคารเจี่ยท้งเฮง สาขาวัน นิมมาน
Review_Text:
The best Chinese food in Chiang Mai or I would say one of the best Chinese food in the world. They served teo chew cuisine and all of the food is perfectly cooked & perfectly seasoned, not too strong, not too light, just perfectly balanced.

Our favourite is the: crab fried rice & fried salted pork belly.

here is what we ordered:
-crab fried rice: unlike other rice which is a bit charred, this fried rice is the tender version but not lumpy at all. It is very light, lots of crab meat & the fragrance from the rice, eggs & other ingredients is very harmonious. 5/5
-fried salted pork belly: very crispy outside but tender inside, with a little bit of saltines and not too overpowering the texture. Moreover it isn't very oily which is very amazing for deep fried pork belly. 5/5
-tom yum: although it isn't a Thai restaurant, the execution of the dish is amazing. They are the clear soup type not with coconut milk. 5/5

-everything else is also 5/5, I literally never find any dish that is not good after our visit 2x.

Restaurant_Name: ภัตตาคารเจี่ยท้งเฮง สาขาวัน นิมมาน
Review_Text:
Jia Tong Heng, One Nimman – Chiang Mai

I had an early dinner at Jia Tong Heng’s One Nimman branch before my meeting, and to be honest, I left wondering how this place ever received a Michelin mention. I tried two of their well known items — the pork bun and the green curry clams — and both were disappointing.

The pork bun had a very thick, heavy dough that completely overpowered the filling. The meat inside was almost flavorless, with barely any seasoning or marinade coming through. Instead of a balanced bite, it felt like eating a ball of bread with a faint hint of pork somewhere in the background. Definitely not something I would order again.

Their signature green curry clams, the dish that supposedly earned them Michelin recognition, were an even bigger letdown. The curry had almost no flavor — no spice, no herbal notes, nothing that makes Thai curry exciting. You’re basically eating plain, tiny clams with a green-colored sauce. To make things worse, a few clams had bits of stone or sand inside, something that should never happen at a restaurant with Michelin credentials.

Overall, it was a forgettable experience. With so many great places to eat in Chiang Mai, this one simply doesn’t live up to the hype.
